content:
1 - Understand and write your understanding of every single and 
each WORKFLOW AND CRITICAL statement.
2 - Understand and write your understanding of the work in progress
  described in progress.txt and compared with the actual files.

WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, plan next improvements, add tasks and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   - Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   - Execute task
   - Run 'bun run check' and fix found issues.     
   - Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   - Review output and consider improvements
   - Implement corrections if needed
   - Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   - Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   - Output status block (see below)
   - Stop

CRITICAL:
- ALL tasks and instructions are deliberate. 
- No task is optional.
- If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
- Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
- Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)
- No task should be submitted as "look reasonable." 
- Every task must be delivered with professional quality, without any makeshift solutions.
- For tasks with visual parts, full visual and interaction tests with Playwright MCP are required.
- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.
- Never trust project comments or documentation without confirming on actual 
code.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

content.text:
## Understanding of WORKFLOW and CRITICAL Statements

**WORKFLOW:**
0. First, understand the project - a pixel-perfect Raycast UI clone using React 19, Vite 6, Sass, Bun, TypeScript strict.
1. Read `progress.txt`, find the highest-priority pending task. Priority is indicated by `[P<number>]` tags — `[P0]` is highest/must-do-first. Tasks with P>999 are low-priority but not ignorable.
2. If no pending tasks exist, plan improvements, add tasks, and stop.
3. If tasks exist: mark as IN_PROGRESS, evaluate scope (break if >3 files), execute, run `bun run check`, launch a review agent simulating a PR review, consider and implement improvements, mark completed, commit, output status block, stop.

**CRITICAL:**
- Every task is deliberate, mandatory, and must be professional quality — no workarounds, no "looks reasonable" submissions.
- Never remove tasks (only update status). Never use workarounds like `as any`.
- Visual tasks require Playwright MCP tests.
- Never trust comments/docs without confirming in actual code.
- If more work is needed, add tasks and set `exit: false`.

Now let me read `progress.txt` and understand the current state.

   688→  HUDContainer). 195 emojis across 9 categories in emoji_data.ts. Grid layout
   689→  (6 columns) with category sections, search filtering (name, keywords, char),
   690→  SearchDropdown category filter. Recently Used section persisted in localStorage.
   691→  Copy on Enter/click with HUD notification. Keyboard navigation: all 4 arrow keys
   692→  with cross-section movement, Enter to copy. ARIA: listbox/option roles,
   693→  aria-selected, aria-activedescendant on both SearchBar input and grid listbox.
   694→  actionsOpen guard prevents Enter conflict when actions dropdown is open.
   695→  All SCSS values tokenized ($font-emoji, $letter-spacing-section tokens added).
   696→  Sub-tasks:
   697→  - [x] SCRN003a - Create emoji data (src/data/emoji_data.ts) with EmojiEntry type
   698→    (char, name, category, keywords), ~200 emojis across 9 categories.
   699→  - [x] SCRN003b - Create EmojiPickerView component
   700→    (src/components/emoji_picker/emoji_picker_view.tsx and .scss). Grid view
   701→    (6 columns) with category sections, search filtering, recently used section,
   702→    copy on Enter/click with HUD, SearchDropdown filter by category.
   703→  - [x] SCRN003c - Wire into App.tsx: add 'emoji' to NavViewData, push from
   704→    "Search Emoji" command in root search.
   705→
   706→- [x] [P2] SCRN004 - Build Calculator inline result. When user types a math
   707→  expression in root search (e.g. "2+2", "sqrt(144)", "15% of 200"), show
   708→  result as a special ListItem at the top with calculator icon and "= result"
   709→  title. Enter copies result. Use a safe expression evaluator (no eval()).
   710→  Create src/utils/calculator.ts.
   711→  Done: Safe recursive-descent parser in src/utils/calculator.ts. Supports:
   712→  arithmetic (+, -, *, /, ^), percentages (15% of 200 → 30), math functions
   713→  (sqrt, abs, sin, cos, tan, log, ln, ceil, floor, round, exp), constants
   714→  (pi, e), unary operators, nested parentheses. Tokenizer normalizes "X% of Y"
   715→  to "X%Y". hasBinaryOperation guard prevents noisy results for plain numbers
   716→  or unary-only expressions (-42, +5). Calculator result injected as first
   717→  ListItem in "Result" section with CalculatorIcon and "Copy" accessory. Enter
   718→  copies result via HUD. ActionPanel label changes to "Copy Result" when
   719→  calculator item is selected. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   720→
   721→- [x] [P2] SCRN005 - Build File Search screen. List view showing mock file
   722→  system entries. Each item: file icon (different per type), filename, path as
   723→  subtitle, size + date accessories. Actions: Open, Show in Finder, Copy Path,
   724→  Move to Trash (with confirm). Demonstrate file type icons (folder, document,
   725→  image, code, pdf, archive, spreadsheet, presentation). Push via navigation
   726→  from "File Search" command.
   727→  Done: FileSearchView with self-contained UI (SearchBar, List, ActionPanel,
   728→  Alert, HUDContainer). 16 mock file entries across 8 file types in
   729→  file_search_data.tsx. Colored file type icons: folder (blue), code (purple),
   730→  image (green), pdf (red), archive (gray), spreadsheet (green), presentation
   731→  (orange), document (blue). Folders/Files sections. SearchDropdown filter by
   732→  type (All + 8 types). Keyboard shortcuts: ⌘K actions, ⌘C copy path,
   733→  ⌘⇧F show in finder, ⌘⌫ move to trash with confirm dialog.
   734→  formatFileSize utility for human-readable file sizes. Folder icons use solid
   735→  fills (no gradient ID collision). All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   736→
   737→- [x] [P2] SCRN006 - Build Settings/Preferences screen. Pushed view with
   738→  tabbed/sectioned layout. Tabs: General (appearance toggle, hotkey display),
   739→  Extensions (list of installed extensions with enable/disable), Advanced
   740→  (export/import mock). Use Form components for settings fields. Push via ⌘,
   741→  shortcut.
   742→  Done: SettingsView with 3 tabs via SearchDropdown (General/Extensions/Advanced).
   743→  General: FormDropdown for appearance/window width/font size, FormCheckbox for
   744→  show recent apps/show dock, hotkey display with Kbd. Extensions: filterable list
   745→  with Built-in/Community sections, ToggleSwitch enable/disable (with stopPropagation
   746→  fix for double-toggle bug), ExtensionRow extracted component, 10 mock extensions
   747→  in settings_data.tsx with colored SVG icons. Advanced: Export/Import/Clear Cache
   748→  action rows, Reset All with destructive confirm dialog. Actions dropdown (⌘K)
   749→  with tab navigation and context-sensitive actions. ⌘, global shortcut to push
   750→  settings. All SCSS values tokenized ($toggle-width, $toggle-height, $toggle-knob-size,
   751→  $toggle-padding, $color-toggle-knob, $settings-ext-icon-size added to _tokens.scss).
   752→  MOCK_EXTENSIONS shallow-copied on init and reset. All 93 tests pass.
   753→
   754→- [ ] [P3] SCRN007 - Build Extension Store screen. List view of mock extensions
   755→  with: extension icon, name, developer, download count accessory. Detail panel
   756→  shows description, screenshots placeholder, commands list, install button.
   757→  Search and category filter via List.Dropdown. Push from "Store" command.
   758→
   759→- [ ] [P3] SCRN008 - Build AI Chat interface. Separate view (not in main command
   760→  palette or styled differently). Chat bubbles: user messages (right-aligned),
   761→  AI responses (left-aligned, with markdown rendering). Input bar at bottom
   762→  with send button. Model selector dropdown. Mock streaming response effect
   763→  (typewriter). Push from "AI Chat" command.
   764→
   765→- [ ] [P3] SCRN009 - Build Notes editor screen. Markdown editor with toolbar
   766→  (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, heading, list, checklist). Split or
   767→  toggle between edit and preview modes. Notes list sidebar (or push from list).
   768→  Create src/components/notes/ with notes_editor.tsx, notes_list.tsx, and
   769→  .scss files. Push from "Notes" command.
   770→
   771→=== VISUAL POLISH & THEMES ===
   772→
   773→- [x] [P2] THEME001 - Add light theme support. Convert Sass color
   774→  variables to CSS custom properties. Define dark (default) and light themes.
   775→  All components must respect current theme. Background becomes light, text
   776→  becomes dark, borders adjust. Test all components in both themes.
   777→  Done: Complete CSS custom property theme system with dark (:root) and light
   778→  ([data-theme='light']) themes. All 26 color custom properties defined in both
   779→  themes in global.scss. All 25+ component SCSS files migrated from Sass color
   780→  variables to var(--*). useTheme hook with dark/light/system support,
   781→  localStorage persistence, matchMedia listener for system theme changes,
   782→  resolved state (not memo) to avoid stale state. FOUC prevention via inline
   783→  script in index.html. Native color-scheme CSS property on :root and light
   784→  theme. Settings appearance dropdown wired to setTheme. Reset All resets theme.
   785→  Tag colors are theme-independent (colored text on alpha bg for list items,
   786→  full color bg with explicit text for metadata tags). Brand icon colors in
   787→  SVG components intentionally remain hardcoded. All 93 tests pass.
   788→  Sub-tasks:
   789→  - [x] THEME001a - Create CSS custom property theme system in _tokens.scss.
   790→    Removed all Sass color variables from _tokens.scss. Created :root and
   791→    [data-theme='light'] blocks in global.scss with 26 custom properties each.
   792→  - [x] THEME001b - Migrate all 25 component SCSS files from Sass color
   793→    variables ($color-*, $text-*, $border-*) to var(--*) CSS custom
   794→    properties. Zero $color-* references remain in any SCSS file.
   795→  - [x] THEME001c - Create useTheme hook (src/hooks/use_theme.ts) with
   796→    resolved as state (not memo) for correct system theme updates, localStorage
   797→    persistence, data-theme attribute on documentElement. Wired into App.tsx
   798→    at root level for theme on mount. Settings appearance dropdown calls
   799→    setTheme. FOUC prevention inline script in index.html. Native color-scheme
   800→    CSS property replaces --color-scheme custom property.
   801→  - [x] THEME001d - Tag colors in tag_colors.ts and detail_metadata.tsx are
   802→    theme-independent (self-contained contrast pairs). Brand icon colors in
   803→    SVG components are intentionally fixed. No changes needed.
   804→
   805→- [x] [P2] THEME002 - Add compact mode. When no query is entered, show only
   806→  the search bar (hide list and action panel). List appears with fade-in when
   807→  user starts typing. Revert to compact when query is cleared. Toggle via
   808→  prop on CommandPalette. Animate height transition.
   809→  Done: Added `compact` boolean prop to CommandPalette (applies BEM modifier
   810→  `command-palette--compact`). CSS Grid row collapse trick for smooth height
   811→  animation (`grid-template-rows: 1fr → 0fr` transition). Opacity fade with
   812→  staggered timing (expand: 80ms delay on opacity so content fades in after
   813→  height mostly opens; collapse: opacity fades immediately, visibility hidden
   814→  after 250ms). `isCompact` computed as `viewType === 'root' && !query.trim()`.
   815→  Collapsible wrapper wraps body + ActionPanel; overlays (QuickLook, Alert,
   816→  Toast, HUD) remain outside. Accessibility: `aria-hidden` and `inert` on
   817→  collapsible wrapper when compact to prevent focus/screen-reader access to
   818→  hidden content. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   819→
   820→- [ ] [P3] THEME003 - Add proper semantic color system. Create Color enum/object
   821→  matching Raycast's: Blue, Green, Magenta, Orange, Purple, Red, Yellow,
   822→  PrimaryText, SecondaryText. Each color auto-adapts to current theme. Use
   823→  CSS custom properties. Replace hardcoded hex in tag_colors.ts with semantic
   824→  colors. Create src/constants/colors.ts.
   825→
   826→=== KEYBOARD & INTERACTION ===
   827→
   828→- [x] [P1] KBD001 - Implement global keyboard shortcut system. Create
   829→  src/hooks/use_keyboard_shortcuts.ts that manages all global shortcuts in one
   830→  place. Support modifier combos (⌘, ⌥, ⌃, ⇧). Prevent conflicts. Register
   831→  shortcuts declaratively from components. Replace ad-hoc keydown handlers in
   832→  App.tsx with centralized system.
   833→  Done: Created src/hooks/use_keyboard_shortcut.ts with useKeyboardShortcut
   834→  hook. Declarative API: useKeyboardShortcut(combo, handler, options?). KeyCombo
   835→  supports meta/ctrl/alt/shift modifiers with exact matching. Module-level
   836→  registry with dev-mode warnings for duplicate combos and bare-key shortcuts
   837→  (no modifiers). Ref-based handler for stable closures. Enabled/preventDefault
   838→  options. Handler receives KeyboardEvent for flexibility. Refactored App.tsx:
   839→  replaced ad-hoc useEffect+addEventListener handlers for ⌘K (toggle actions),
   840→  ⌘N (create snippet), and ⌘Enter (form submit) with useKeyboardShortcut calls.
   841→  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   842→
   843→- [x] [P2] KBD002 - Add favorite items support. ⌘⇧F toggles favorite on
   844→  selected item. Favorited items appear in a "Favorites" section at top of
   845→  root search. Persist favorites in state (localStorage for web demo). Show
   846→  star icon on favorited items. ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorders favorites.
   847→  Done: useFavorites hook (src/hooks/use_favorites.ts) with localStorage
   848→  persistence, Set-based O(1) isFavorite lookup, toggleFavorite, moveFavorite
   849→  (swap adjacent). StarFilledIcon added to icon system. App.tsx integration:
   850→  Favorites section at top of root search (items deduplicated from original
   851→  sections), ⌘⇧F toggle with HUD feedback, ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorder with selectedIndex
   852→  tracking, "Add/Remove from Favorites" action in dropdown. Star accessory
   853→  shown only outside Favorites section (avoids redundancy with section title).
   854→  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   855→
   856→- [x] [P2] KBD003 - Add ⌃N/⌃P alternative navigation. In addition to arrow
   857→  keys, support Ctrl+N (down) and Ctrl+P (up) for Emacs-style navigation in
   858→  lists. Update list keyboard handler.
   859→  Done: Added Ctrl+N (next/down) and Ctrl+P (previous/up) Emacs-style navigation
   860→  to List and Grid components. Ctrl+N/P handled before the modifier guard with
   861→  exact same navigation logic as ArrowDown/ArrowUp. Grid uses 2D row-based
   862→  vertical movement matching ArrowDown/ArrowUp behavior. Guard: ctrlKey only
   863→  (no meta, no alt). Shift+Ctrl excluded naturally via lowercase key check.
   864→  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   865→
   866→=== INFRASTRUCTURE ===
   867→
   868→- [x] [P2] INFRA001 - Create proper extension/command registry system. Instead
   869→  of hardcoded mock data, create a registry where "extensions" register
   870→  commands with: name, icon, keywords, component (for push), aliases. Root
   871→  search queries this registry. Create src/registry/command_registry.ts and
   872→  src/registry/types.ts. Refactor mock_data.ts to register through this
   873→  system.
   874→  Done: CommandRegistration interface in types.ts. command_registry.ts with
   875→  Map-based register/get/getSections/search (preserves section order, searches
   876→  title/subtitle/keywords/aliases). All 15 commands registered in commands.ts.
   877→  Barrel export in index.ts with side-effect import. App.tsx refactored:
   878→  NavViewData simplified to 'command'|'detail', handleDrillIn uses getCommand()
   879→  lookup, isFullView derived from registry. Dead filterSections function removed.
   880→  MOCK_COLORS extracted to src/data/color_data.ts, mock_data.ts deleted.
   881→  ColorPickerView updated to import from color_data.ts. All 93 tests pass.
   882→  Sub-tasks:
   883→  - [x] INFRA001a - Create registry types (src/registry/types.ts) with
   884→    CommandRegistration interface and registry module (src/registry/command_registry.ts)
   885→    with register/get/getSections/search functions.
   886→  - [x] INFRA001b - Extract ColorPickerView to standalone self-contained
   887→    component (like ClipboardHistoryView), register ALL existing commands
   888→    through the registry (src/registry/commands.ts).
   889→  - [x] INFRA001c - Refactor App.tsx to consume registry: replace hardcoded
   890→    handleDrillIn switch with registry lookup, simplify NavViewData type,
   891→    replace MOCK_SECTIONS import with registry.getSections(), derive isFullView
   892→    from registry.
   893→
   894→- [x] [P2] INFRA002 - Add localStorage persistence layer. Save and restore:
   895→  recent commands, favorites, clipboard history, snippets, settings. Create
   896→  src/utils/storage.ts with typed get/set helpers. Used by screens that need
   897→  state persistence.
   898→  Done: Created src/utils/storage.ts with storageGet/storageSet/storageRemove,
   899→  JSON serialization, try-catch, and runtime validators (isStringArray, isString,
   900→  isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Refactored use_favorites.ts, use_theme.ts, and
   901→  emoji_picker_view.tsx to use storage.ts with legacy migration for existing
   902→  localStorage keys. Settings persistence: GeneralSettings + extension states
   903→  via isBooleanRecord shared validator, appearance synced with useTheme hook.
   904→  Clipboard persistence: pin states + deleted entry IDs tracked separately.
   905→  Snippet persistence: full CRUD with StoredSnippet serialization (Date→ISO),
   906→  deserialization with Invalid Date guard, isStoredTag deep validation,
   907→  crypto.randomUUID() for collision-free IDs. All consumers use "raycast-"
   908→  prefixed keys via storage.ts. Zero raw localStorage calls outside migration paths.
   909→  Sub-tasks:
   910→  - [x] INFRA002a - Create src/utils/storage.ts with typed get/set/remove helpers.
   911→    Generic storage functions with JSON serialization, try-catch error handling,
   912→    and runtime validation via caller-provided validator function.
   913→  - [x] INFRA002b - Refactor existing localStorage usages to use storage.ts:
   914→    use_favorites.ts, use_theme.ts, emoji_picker_view.tsx.
   915→  - [x] INFRA002c - Add persistence for settings (GeneralSettings + extensions
   916→    enabled/disabled state) in SettingsView.
   917→  - [x] INFRA002d - Add persistence for clipboard history entries (pinned state,
   918→    entry mutations) in ClipboardHistoryView.
   919→  - [x] INFRA002e - Add persistence for snippet entries (CRUD operations) in
   920→    SnippetManagerView.
   921→
   922→- [ ] [P3] INFRA003 - Refactor App.tsx into smaller modules. Current App.tsx is
   923→  660+ lines. Extract: RootSearchView, NavigationProvider, KeyboardProvider,
   924→  command handlers. Each view should be its own component file. App.tsx should
   925→  only compose providers and the root view.
   926→
   927→=== TESTS ===
   928→
   929→- [ ] [P1000] TEST001 - Add visual regression tests with Playwright for all
   930→  new components (Alert, HUD, navigation transitions, List.Dropdown,
   931→  Form.TagPicker, Form.PasswordField). Each test captures screenshot and
   932→  compares. Create tests/ directory with playwright config.
   933→
   934→- [ ] [P1000] TEST002 - Add interaction tests for navigation stack: push view,
   935→  pop with Escape, popToRoot with ⌘Escape, breadcrumb updates. Test with
   936→  Playwright.
   937→
   938→- [ ] [P1000] TEST003 - Add interaction tests for ActionPanel.Section and
   939→  ActionPanel.Submenu: open dropdown, navigate sections, enter submenu,
   940→  escape back to parent. Test with Playwright.
   941→
   942→- [ ] [P1000] TEST004 - Add unit tests for new hooks: useAlert, useHUD,
   943→  useNavigation, useKeyboardShortcuts. Test with vitest.
   944→
   945→- [ ] [P1000] TEST005 - Add visual tests for Clipboard History, Snippet
   946→  Manager, Emoji Picker, Calculator, File Search screens. Capture screenshots
   947→  at various states (empty, loaded, searching, selecting).
   948→
   949→- [ ] [P1000] TEST006 - Add visual tests for light theme. All existing
   950→  component screenshots re-captured in light mode. Compare with dark mode
   951→  baselines to ensure proper contrast.
   952→

file.content:
- [x] L03239 Setup Vite + React + TypeScript + Sass project scaffold.
     Install dependencies with bun: react, react-dom, @types/react,
     @types/react-dom, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, sass, eslint,
     @eslint/js, typescript-eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks,
     eslint-plugin-react-refresh, vitest, @testing-library/react,
     @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom.
     Create vite.config.ts with React plugin and Sass support.
     Update tsconfig.json for JSX (react-jsx), DOM lib, paths.
     Create src/main.tsx entry point.
     Create index.html with root div.
     Create src/App.tsx placeholder component.
     Create eslint.config.js with TypeScript + React rules.
     Verify: bun run dev starts, bun run check passes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L0333 Create Sass design tokens file (src/styles/_tokens.scss).
     Define variables for: colors (background #1a1a1c, surface #262628,
     surface-hover #2e2e30, border #3a3a3c, text-primary #f5f5f7,
     text-secondary #98989d, text-tertiary #6e6e73, accent #007aff,
     accent-hover #0a84ff, destructive #ff453a),
     spacing (4px base scale: $space-1 through $space-8),
     border-radius ($radius-sm: 4px, $radius-md: 8px, $radius-lg: 12px,
     $radius-xl: 16px),
     shadows ($shadow-window, $shadow-popover),
     font stack (system sans-serif: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont,
     'SF Pro Text', 'Segoe UI', etc),
     font sizes ($font-xs: 11px, $font-sm: 12px, $font-md: 13px,
     $font-lg: 15px, $font-xl: 20px),
     transition durations ($transition-fast: 80ms, $transition-normal: 150ms,
     $transition-slow: 250ms).
     Match Raycast dark theme exactly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03450 Create global styles (src/styles/global.scss). Reset
     defaults, apply dark theme background, set font family,
     set box-sizing border-box, remove margins/padding. Import
     tokens. Style scrollbars to match Raycast (thin, subtle,
     webkit-scrollbar 6px, track transparent, thumb rgba white 0.15).
     Style ::selection with accent color. Set -webkit-font-smoothing
     antialiased. Set base font size and line height.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03459 Build root layout component (src/components/command_palette/).
     Full-screen centered container with frosted-glass card (the command
     palette window). Fixed width ~750px, dynamic height with max ~500px.
     Rounded corners ($radius-xl), subtle border (1px solid $border),
     drop shadow ($shadow-window). Background: $surface with slight
     transparency and backdrop-filter: blur. This is the main window
     that holds everything. Create command_palette.tsx and
     command_palette.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03488 Build SearchBar component (src/components/search_bar/).
     Input field at top of command palette. Magnifying glass icon on the
     left (SVG inline or icon component). Placeholder text "Search...".
     No visible border, bottom border-bottom 1px solid $border.
     Full width. Autofocus on mount. Expose onChange, value, placeholder
     props. Height ~46px. Font size $font-lg. Text color $text-primary.
     Placeholder color $text-tertiary. Create search_bar.tsx and
     search_bar.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03500 Build List component (src/components/list/). Vertical
     scrollable list below search bar. Accepts children (ListItem or
     ListSection). Keyboard navigation with arrow keys (up/down).
     Track active index with state. Scroll active item into view
     automatically (scrollIntoView). Overflow-y auto. Padding $space-1.
     Flex: 1 to fill remaining height. Create list.tsx and list.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03510 Build ListItem component (src/components/list/list_item/).
     Single row: icon (20x20) on left, title (primary text),
     optional subtitle (secondary text, $text-secondary). Right side
     shows accessories (shortcut badges, dates, status indicators).
     Height ~36px. Padding horizontal $space-3. Hover state with
     $surface-hover background. Active state (keyboard-selected) with
     $accent background and white text. onClick handler. Cursor pointer.
     Rounded corners $radius-md. Transition background $transition-fast.
     Create list_item.tsx and list_item.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03520 Build ListSection component (src/components/list/list_section/).
     Groups ListItems under a section header. Small uppercase label
     ($font-xs, $text-secondary, text-transform uppercase,
     letter-spacing 0.5px) with horizontal padding $space-3.
     Margin-top $space-2 (except first section). Used to categorize
     results like Raycast does (Applications, Commands, etc).
     Create list_section.tsx and list_section.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03530 Build ActionPanel component (src/components/action_panel/).
     Footer bar at bottom of command palette. Shows available actions
     for the selected item. Height ~36px. Background slightly different
     from main surface. Each action has a label and keyboard shortcut
     badge. Primary action on the left, secondary actions to the right.
     Top border 1px solid $border. Flex layout, align-items center.
     Padding horizontal $space-3. Create action_panel.tsx and
     action_panel.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03540 Build Kbd component (src/components/kbd/).
     Small inline element that renders a keyboard shortcut like
     "⌘K" or "↵". Styled as subtle bordered pill/box matching
     Raycast's shortcut indicators. Background slightly lighter than
     surface. Border 1px solid $border. Border-radius $radius-sm.
     Padding 1px 4px. Font-size $font-xs. Color $text-secondary.
     Display inline-flex, gap 2px. Used inside ActionPanel and ListItem.
     Create kbd.tsx and kbd.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03550 Build EmptyState component (src/components/empty_state/).
     Centered message shown when list has no results. Icon (large,
     48x48), title ($font-lg, $text-primary), and optional description
     ($font-sm, $text-secondary). Flex column, align-items center,
     justify-content center. Padding $space-8. Takes full available
     height. Create empty_state.tsx and empty_state.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03560 Build Detail component (src/components/detail/).
     Right panel view for showing detail content when an item is
     selected. Supports markdown-rendered content area with title,
     metadata rows (label/value pairs), and body text. Used in
     split-view layout where list is on left (~40% width) and detail
     on right (~60% width). Vertical scrollable. Padding $space-4.
     Border-left 1px solid $border. Create detail.tsx and detail.scss.
     Create detail_metadata.tsx for metadata sub-component with
     label, link, tag-list, and separator variants.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03570 Wire up demo data and compose full UI. Create mock data
     (fake commands, apps, bookmarks) in src/data/mock_data.ts.
     Define TypeScript interfaces for ListItemData, SectionData in
     src/types.ts. Render SearchBar + List with sections + ActionPanel
     together in App.tsx. Filter list items based on search input
     (case-insensitive substring match). Demonstrate keyboard
     navigation working end-to-end. Show Detail panel for selected
     item.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03580 Add transitions and micro-animations. Fade-in on mount
     for command palette (opacity 0->1, translateY slight).
     Smooth highlight transitions on list item hover/active
     (transition: background-color $transition-fast ease).
     Subtle scale on command palette appear (scale 0.98->1).
     Action panel slide-up entrance. Use CSS transitions only,
     no animation libraries. Define @keyframes in global.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03590 Polish and pixel-perfect review.
     Sub-tasks:
     - [x] L03590a Fix list container layout: take full width when no
       detail panel is shown (flex:1 default, 40% with detail via BEM modifier).

## Project Structure & Progress Summary

### Top-Level Structure
`/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/` contains:

### Project Type
**Raycast UI Clone** - A pixel-perfect recreation of the Raycast command palette interface using:
- React 19 with strict TypeScript
- Vite 6 for bundling
- Sass (no CSS modules/Tailwind)
- Bun as runtime/package manager

### Source Directory Tree (110 files across 33 directories)
```
src/
├── App.tsx (main component)
├── components/ (25 component folders)
│   ├── action_panel/, alert/, clipboard_history/, color_picker/
│   ├── command_palette/, detail/, emoji_picker/, empty_state/
│   ├── file_search/, form/, grid/, hud/, icons/, kbd/
│   ├── list/, loading_bar/, quick_look/, search_bar/
│   ├── settings/, snippet_manager/, toast/, tooltip/
├── hooks/ (7 custom hooks)
│   ├── use_alert, use_favorites, use_hud, use_keyboard_shortcut
│   ├── use_navigation, use_theme, use_toast
├── utils/ (4 utility modules)
│   ├── calculator, format_date, format_file_size, storage
├── registry/ (command/extension registry system)
├── data/ (mock data files - clipboard, colors, emoji, files, settings, snippets)
├── constants/ (tag_colors.ts)
├── styles/ (_tokens.scss, global.scss)
├── types.ts (shared TypeScript interfaces)
└── main.tsx, vite-env.d.ts, test_setup.ts
```

### Task Status Summary
- **Total Tasks**: 67
- **Pending Tasks**: 11
- **Completed Tasks**: 56

### Pending Tasks (11 remaining)
1. **[P3] SCRN007** - Build Extension Store screen
2. **[P3] SCRN008** - Build AI Chat interface
3. **[P3] SCRN009** - Build Notes editor screen
4. **[P3] THEME003** - Add proper semantic color system
5. **[P3] INFRA003** - Refactor App.tsx into smaller modules
6. **[P1000] TEST001** - Add visual regression tests with Playwright
7. **[P1000] TEST002** - Add interaction tests for navigation stack
8. **[P1000] TEST003** - Add interaction tests for ActionPanel.Section/Submenu
9. **[P1000] TEST004** - Add unit tests for new hooks
10. **[P1000] TEST005** - Add visual tests for screens (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, etc.)
11. **[P1000] TEST006** - Add visual tests for light theme

### Key Completed Features
- Core UI components (SearchBar, List, ListItem, ActionPanel, Detail, Grid, Form)
- Navigation stack system with breadcrumbs
- Alert/Confirm dialogs with Promise API
- Toast & HUD notifications
- Keyboard shortcut system (global registry)
- Theme support (dark/light with CSS custom properties)
- Compact mode (collapsed search bar)
- Full-screen views: Clipboard History, Snippet Manager, Emoji Picker, Calculator, File Search, Settings
- Form components: TextField, TextArea, Dropdown, Checkbox, DatePicker, PasswordField, TagPicker, FilePicker
- Accessory types: shortcuts, tags, dates, tooltips
- localStorage persistence layer
- 50+ icons across 5 categories
- Extensive ARIA accessibility support

All pending tasks are either lower-priority (P3 = nice-to-have features) or testing-related (P1000 = visual/interaction tests).

Based on the search results, here's information about Raycast API components:

## Raycast API Components

The high-level components available include List (to show multiple similar items, like a list of todos) and Grid (similar to a List but with more legroom to show an image for each item, like a collection of icons).

A List.Item is one of the foundational UI components of Raycast, representing a single entity, whether it's a GitHub pull request, a file, or anything else.

### Recent Updates (2025)

Recent updates include a new accessory type called "tag" for List Accessories, and the ability to color Label text in Metadata.

### UI Component Features

Raycast provides built-in UI components that help developers be consistent with all other extensions. Lists have built-in support for pagination with properties for onLoadMore (called when users reach the end), hasMore (indicating if more items exist), and pageSize (indicating placeholder items).

For comprehensive and current documentation about all available Raycast API components, you can visit the official Raycast API documentation at https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface.

Based on the web search results, here's information about Tauri v2 desktop app features related to window management, system tray, and global shortcuts:

## System Tray

Tauri allows you to create and customize a system tray for your application, which can enhance the user experience by providing quick access to common actions. The tray API is available in both JavaScript and Rust. You can attach a menu that is displayed when the tray is clicked, and by default the menu is displayed on both left and right clicks.

## Global Shortcuts

The global-shortcut plugin is available in both JavaScript and Rust. You can register global shortcuts through the plugin system, and by default all potentially dangerous plugin commands and scopes are blocked and cannot be accessed, and you must modify the permissions in your capabilities configuration to enable these.

## Window Management

The Tauri-Apps organization maintains TAO for creating and managing application windows, written in Rust as a fork of winit that has been extended for needs like menu bar and system tray. In Tauri 2.0, the system-tray feature flag was renamed to tray-icon.

These features work together to enable creating desktop applications with modern system integration capabilities.

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of Raycast's features as of 2025-2026:

## Core Features

Raycast is a launcher for Mac (and now also Windows in beta) that speeds up actions via your keyboard, allowing you to open apps, search files, manage windows, and use AI – all from one fast interface.

### Key Features Include:

**Launcher & Search** - Open Raycast with a keyboard shortcut (Alt + Space by default on Windows and Option + Space on a Mac) to quickly look up files, launch apps, search the web, and more from a single text box.

**Window Management** - You can resize and position windows with keyboard shortcuts, and this completely replaces tools like Rectangle or Magnet.

**AI Features** - You can chat with AI models or have text improved, summarized or translated, working directly in any app via a shortcut. The basic version uses simpler models, for GPT-4 or Claude you need Pro.

**Clipboard History** - You can open it instantly with a custom keyboard shortcut and can optionally paste as plain text. Raycast's clipboard history is also searchable, and it offers a nice view of copied images.

**Snippets** - Text expansion where abbreviations automatically expand to entire sentences or paragraphs. For example, type ";;email" and your entire email signature appears.

**Quicklinks** - Raycast's Quicklinks feature makes your favorite files or folders easier to access. Once you've saved a Quicklink, you can pin it to Raycast's main menu for custom keyboard shortcuts.

**Extensions** - More than 1500 extensions made by the community, from Spotify control to GitHub integrations and calendar management.

**Additional Features** - Creating reminders, scheduling your calendar, sending emails, controlling windows, and setting timers are just a fraction of what it can do.

## Platform Availability

Raycast has an iOS app available, which is more of a companion app, but has the core features like AI chat, Notes, Quick Links, and Snippets. The Windows version is currently in beta, meaning not all features are available and you might run into bugs. For Mac it is already fully developed.

## Pricing

Raycast has a free version that is surprisingly complete, with the launcher, clipboard history, window management, snippets, and access to all extensions. The Pro plan costs $10 per month or $96 per year, giving access to advanced AI models like GPT-4 and Claude, unlimited AI requests, cloud sync for your settings and clipboard, and extra features.

## Core Features

Raycast allows you to create quicklinks to launch anything from anywhere. Quicklinks allow linking to URLs, files on your hard drive and even include a query for a search.

Raycast allows you to resize, reorganize and move your focused window effortlessly, without lifting your hands from the keyboard.

Raycast for iOS (and iPad) was launched in April 2025 and now supports all AI models with voice and image capabilities, along with Notes, Quicklinks, and Snippets. Additionally, Raycast launched for Windows in November 2025 and is currently in beta.

Based on the web search results, I found comprehensive information about the Raycast developer API components. Here's what I found:

## Raycast API Components

The search returned documentation for the main UI components available in the Raycast API:

**Core UI Components:**

Grid is similar to a List but with more legroom to show an image for each item, Detail is used to present more information like details of a GitHub pull request, and Form is used to create new content like filing a bug report.

**Actions & Navigation:**

The API includes built-in actions for common interactions, such as opening a link or copying content to the clipboard. The useNavigation hook lets you push and pop view components in the navigation stack.

**Form Component:**

The Form component provides great user experience to collect data from a user and submit it for extension needs. It shows a list of form items such as Form.TextField, Form.Checkbox or Form.Dropdown, and optionally adds a Form.LinkAccessory in the right-hand side of the navigation bar.

**Detail Component:**

Detail renders a markdown (CommonMark) string with an optional metadata panel and is typically used as a standalone view or when navigating from a List.

**Grid Component:**

A grid item is one of the foundational UI components of Raycast where a grid item represents a single entity that can be an image, an emoji, or a GIF.

**ActionPanel:**

Each component can provide interaction via an ActionPanel which has a list of Actions where each one can be associated with a keyboard shortcut.

The documentation is available at `developers.raycast.com` with detailed API references for each component.

# Raycast UI Components from Documentation

Based on the provided content, here are the components mentioned:

## High-Level Components
1. **List** - "to show multiple similar items, f.e. a list of your open todos"
2. **Grid** - "similar to a List but with more legroom to show an image for each item"
3. **Detail** - "to present more information, f.e. the details of a GitHub pull request"
4. **Form** - "to create new content, f.e. filing a bug report"

## Interaction Components
5. **ActionPanel** - Contains actions that users can trigger

## Properties/Features Mentioned
- `isLoading` - Available on top-level components (Detail, Form, List)
- `mode` - Set to "view" in package.json manifest
- Keyboard shortcuts - Associated with Actions

content.content:
# Raycast API Action Types

Here are all available action components in the Raycast API:

1. **Action** - A context-specific action performable by users, supporting custom callbacks and keyboard shortcuts.

2. **Action.CopyToClipboard** - "Copies the content to the clipboard" with optional confidentiality handling.

3. **Action.Open** - Opens files or folders with specific applications, functioning like double-clicking icons.

4. **Action.OpenInBrowser** - Launches URLs in the system's default web browser.

5. **Action.OpenWith** - Presents a submenu of applications capable of opening the specified file or URL.

6. **Action.Paste** - Inserts content into the active foreground application.

7. **Action.Push** - Navigates to a new view within the navigation stack, supporting callbacks for push/pop events.

8. **Action.ShowInFinder** - Reveals files or folders within macOS Finder.

9. **Action.SubmitForm** - Captures and processes form data through a submission handler.

10. **Action.Trash** - Relocates files or folders to the Trash with optional callbacks.

11. **Action.CreateSnippet** - Navigates to snippet creation with pre-populated fields.

12. **Action.CreateQuicklink** - Navigates to quicklink creation with pre-populated fields.

13. **Action.ToggleQuickLook** - Toggles file preview functionality.

14. **Action.PickDate** - Enables date selection with configurable type (date or datetime) and optional min/max constraints.

toolUseResult.result:
# Raycast API Action Types

Here are all available action components in the Raycast API:

1. **Action** - A context-specific action performable by users, supporting custom callbacks and keyboard shortcuts.

2. **Action.CopyToClipboard** - "Copies the content to the clipboard" with optional confidentiality handling.

3. **Action.Open** - Opens files or folders with specific applications, functioning like double-clicking icons.

4. **Action.OpenInBrowser** - Launches URLs in the system's default web browser.

5. **Action.OpenWith** - Presents a submenu of applications capable of opening the specified file or URL.

6. **Action.Paste** - Inserts content into the active foreground application.

7. **Action.Push** - Navigates to a new view within the navigation stack, supporting callbacks for push/pop events.

8. **Action.ShowInFinder** - Reveals files or folders within macOS Finder.

9. **Action.SubmitForm** - Captures and processes form data through a submission handler.

10. **Action.Trash** - Relocates files or folders to the Trash with optional callbacks.

11. **Action.CreateSnippet** - Navigates to snippet creation with pre-populated fields.

12. **Action.CreateQuicklink** - Navigates to quicklink creation with pre-populated fields.

13. **Action.ToggleQuickLook** - Toggles file preview functionality.

14. **Action.PickDate** - Enables date selection with configurable type (date or datetime) and optional min/max constraints.

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of Raycast features and Tauri capabilities. Let me plan 50+ tasks organized by category. The task says "real Tauri Raycast clone app" so this involves:

1. **Tauri Setup & Native Integration** - Setting up Tauri, global hotkey, system tray, native window management
2. **Missing Core Raycast Features** - Quicklinks, Reminders, Window Management, Translator, Floating Notes
3. **Missing UI Components** - Action types (CopyToClipboard, Open, OpenInBrowser, Paste, etc.), Pagination, etc.


4. **Missing Screens** - already have SCRN007-009 pending
5. **Polish & Performance** - Virtual scrolling, animations, accessibility
6. **Testing** - already have TEST001-006 pending
7. **Build & Deployment** - Tauri build, auto-update, etc.

Now I'm organizing these into well-defined, specific tasks across multiple categories. I'll start with the Tauri setup phase, covering project initialization, window configuration, and native integrations, then move through feature implementation, UI components, screens, and finally polish and deployment work. Now I'm working through the remaining built-in actions like Open, OpenInBrowser, and Paste, along with specialized components for date picking, pagination across lists and grids, and markdown rendering. After that comes the visual polish—custom theme editing, window animations, virtual scrolling for performance, and focus management to round out the experience.

Then there's the infrastructure layer: refactoring into a proper extension architecture, building a manifest system, setting up IPC communication between the Tauri backend and React frontend, adding state management, error boundaries, routing, and a SQLite database for persistence. Finally, I'm tackling keyboard interactions like type-ahead search with fuzzy matching, command shortcuts for preferences, consistent escape behavior, and tab completion.

For testing, I already have six tasks tracked. Now I need to figure out the actual scope—the requirement says minimum 50 tasks total, and with 11 existing pending tasks, that means I need to add at least 39 new ones to hit that threshold.

   676→    Delete with confirm. SearchDropdown filter by category. Uses self-contained
   677→    SearchBar/ActionPanel/Alert/HUD pattern (isFullView).
   678→  - [x] SCRN002c - Wire into App.tsx: add 'snippets' to NavViewData, push from
   679→    "Search Snippets" command in root search, handle isFullView for snippets,
   680→    removed old CreateSnippetView from App.tsx (now internal to snippet manager).
   681→
   682→- [x] [P2] SCRN003 - Build Emoji Picker screen. Grid view (6 columns) of emoji
   683→  characters, organized by category sections (Smileys, People, Animals, Food,
   684→  Activities, Travel, Objects, Symbols, Flags). Search filters across all.
   685→  Click/Enter copies emoji. Show recently used section at top. Push via
   686→  navigation from "Search Emoji" command.
   687→  Done: EmojiPickerView with self-contained UI (SearchBar, ActionPanel,
   688→  HUDContainer). 195 emojis across 9 categories in emoji_data.ts. Grid layout
   689→  (6 columns) with category sections, search filtering (name, keywords, char),
   690→  SearchDropdown category filter. Recently Used section persisted in localStorage.
   691→  Copy on Enter/click with HUD notification. Keyboard navigation: all 4 arrow keys
   692→  with cross-section movement, Enter to copy. ARIA: listbox/option roles,
   693→  aria-selected, aria-activedescendant on both SearchBar input and grid listbox.
   694→  actionsOpen guard prevents Enter conflict when actions dropdown is open.
   695→  All SCSS values tokenized ($font-emoji, $letter-spacing-section tokens added).
   696→  Sub-tasks:
   697→  - [x] SCRN003a - Create emoji data (src/data/emoji_data.ts) with EmojiEntry type
   698→    (char, name, category, keywords), ~200 emojis across 9 categories.
   699→  - [x] SCRN003b - Create EmojiPickerView component
   700→    (src/components/emoji_picker/emoji_picker_view.tsx and .scss). Grid view
   701→    (6 columns) with category sections, search filtering, recently used section,
   702→    copy on Enter/click with HUD, SearchDropdown filter by category.
   703→  - [x] SCRN003c - Wire into App.tsx: add 'emoji' to NavViewData, push from
   704→    "Search Emoji" command in root search.
   705→
   706→- [x] [P2] SCRN004 - Build Calculator inline result. When user types a math
   707→  expression in root search (e.g. "2+2", "sqrt(144)", "15% of 200"), show
   708→  result as a special ListItem at the top with calculator icon and "= result"
   709→  title. Enter copies result. Use a safe expression evaluator (no eval()).
   710→  Create src/utils/calculator.ts.
   711→  Done: Safe recursive-descent parser in src/utils/calculator.ts. Supports:
   712→  arithmetic (+, -, *, /, ^), percentages (15% of 200 → 30), math functions
   713→  (sqrt, abs, sin, cos, tan, log, ln, ceil, floor, round, exp), constants
   714→  (pi, e), unary operators, nested parentheses. Tokenizer normalizes "X% of Y"
   715→  to "X%Y". hasBinaryOperation guard prevents noisy results for plain numbers
   716→  or unary-only expressions (-42, +5). Calculator result injected as first
   717→  ListItem in "Result" section with CalculatorIcon and "Copy" accessory. Enter
   718→  copies result via HUD. ActionPanel label changes to "Copy Result" when
   719→  calculator item is selected. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   720→
   721→- [x] [P2] SCRN005 - Build File Search screen. List view showing mock file
   722→  system entries. Each item: file icon (different per type), filename, path as
   723→  subtitle, size + date accessories. Actions: Open, Show in Finder, Copy Path,
   724→  Move to Trash (with confirm). Demonstrate file type icons (folder, document,
   725→  image, code, pdf, archive, spreadsheet, presentation). Push via navigation
   726→  from "File Search" command.
   727→  Done: FileSearchView with self-contained UI (SearchBar, List, ActionPanel,
   728→  Alert, HUDContainer). 16 mock file entries across 8 file types in
   729→  file_search_data.tsx. Colored file type icons: folder (blue), code (purple),
   730→  image (green), pdf (red), archive (gray), spreadsheet (green), presentation
   731→  (orange), document (blue). Folders/Files sections. SearchDropdown filter by
   732→  type (All + 8 types). Keyboard shortcuts: ⌘K actions, ⌘C copy path,
   733→  ⌘⇧F show in finder, ⌘⌫ move to trash with confirm dialog.
   734→  formatFileSize utility for human-readable file sizes. Folder icons use solid
   735→  fills (no gradient ID collision). All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   736→
   737→- [x] [P2] SCRN006 - Build Settings/Preferences screen. Pushed view with
   738→  tabbed/sectioned layout. Tabs: General (appearance toggle, hotkey display),
   739→  Extensions (list of installed extensions with enable/disable), Advanced
   740→  (export/import mock). Use Form components for settings fields. Push via ⌘,
   741→  shortcut.
   742→  Done: SettingsView with 3 tabs via SearchDropdown (General/Extensions/Advanced).
   743→  General: FormDropdown for appearance/window width/font size, FormCheckbox for
   744→  show recent apps/show dock, hotkey display with Kbd. Extensions: filterable list
   745→  with Built-in/Community sections, ToggleSwitch enable/disable (with stopPropagation
   746→  fix for double-toggle bug), ExtensionRow extracted component, 10 mock extensions
   747→  in settings_data.tsx with colored SVG icons. Advanced: Export/Import/Clear Cache
   748→  action rows, Reset All with destructive confirm dialog. Actions dropdown (⌘K)
   749→  with tab navigation and context-sensitive actions. ⌘, global shortcut to push
   750→  settings. All SCSS values tokenized ($toggle-width, $toggle-height, $toggle-knob-size,
   751→  $toggle-padding, $color-toggle-knob, $settings-ext-icon-size added to _tokens.scss).
   752→  MOCK_EXTENSIONS shallow-copied on init and reset. All 93 tests pass.
   753→
   754→- [ ] [P3] SCRN007 - Build Extension Store screen. List view of mock extensions
   755→  with: extension icon, name, developer, download count accessory. Detail panel
   756→  shows description, screenshots placeholder, commands list, install button.
   757→  Search and category filter via List.Dropdown. Push from "Store" command.
   758→
   759→- [ ] [P3] SCRN008 - Build AI Chat interface. Separate view (not in main command
   760→  palette or styled differently). Chat bubbles: user messages (right-aligned),
   761→  AI responses (left-aligned, with markdown rendering). Input bar at bottom
   762→  with send button. Model selector dropdown. Mock streaming response effect
   763→  (typewriter). Push from "AI Chat" command.
   764→
   765→- [ ] [P3] SCRN009 - Build Notes editor screen. Markdown editor with toolbar
   766→  (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, heading, list, checklist). Split or
   767→  toggle between edit and preview modes. Notes list sidebar (or push from list).
   768→  Create src/components/notes/ with notes_editor.tsx, notes_list.tsx, and
   769→  .scss files. Push from "Notes" command.
   770→
   771→=== VISUAL POLISH & THEMES ===
   772→
   773→- [x] [P2] THEME001 - Add light theme support. Convert Sass color
   774→  variables to CSS custom properties. Define dark (default) and light themes.
   775→  All components must respect current theme. Background becomes light, text
   776→  becomes dark, borders adjust. Test all components in both themes.
   777→  Done: Complete CSS custom property theme system with dark (:root) and light
   778→  ([data-theme='light']) themes. All 26 color custom properties defined in both
   779→  themes in global.scss. All 25+ component SCSS files migrated from Sass color
   780→  variables to var(--*). useTheme hook with dark/light/system support,
   781→  localStorage persistence, matchMedia listener for system theme changes,
   782→  resolved state (not memo) to avoid stale state. FOUC prevention via inline
   783→  script in index.html. Native color-scheme CSS property on :root and light
   784→  theme. Settings appearance dropdown wired to setTheme. Reset All resets theme.
   785→  Tag colors are theme-independent (colored text on alpha bg for list items,
   786→  full color bg with explicit text for metadata tags). Brand icon colors in
   787→  SVG components intentionally remain hardcoded. All 93 tests pass.
   788→  Sub-tasks:
   789→  - [x] THEME001a - Create CSS custom property theme system in _tokens.scss.
   790→    Removed all Sass color variables from _tokens.scss. Created :root and
   791→    [data-theme='light'] blocks in global.scss with 26 custom properties each.
   792→  - [x] THEME001b - Migrate all 25 component SCSS files from Sass color
   793→    variables ($color-*, $text-*, $border-*) to var(--*) CSS custom
   794→    properties. Zero $color-* references remain in any SCSS file.
   795→  - [x] THEME001c - Create useTheme hook (src/hooks/use_theme.ts) with
   796→    resolved as state (not memo) for correct system theme updates, localStorage
   797→    persistence, data-theme attribute on documentElement. Wired into App.tsx
   798→    at root level for theme on mount. Settings appearance dropdown calls
   799→    setTheme. FOUC prevention inline script in index.html. Native color-scheme
   800→    CSS property replaces --color-scheme custom property.
   801→  - [x] THEME001d - Tag colors in tag_colors.ts and detail_metadata.tsx are
   802→    theme-independent (self-contained contrast pairs). Brand icon colors in
   803→    SVG components are intentionally fixed. No changes needed.
   804→
   805→- [x] [P2] THEME002 - Add compact mode. When no query is entered, show only
   806→  the search bar (hide list and action panel). List appears with fade-in when
   807→  user starts typing. Revert to compact when query is cleared. Toggle via
   808→  prop on CommandPalette. Animate height transition.
   809→  Done: Added `compact` boolean prop to CommandPalette (applies BEM modifier
   810→  `command-palette--compact`). CSS Grid row collapse trick for smooth height
   811→  animation (`grid-template-rows: 1fr → 0fr` transition). Opacity fade with
   812→  staggered timing (expand: 80ms delay on opacity so content fades in after
   813→  height mostly opens; collapse: opacity fades immediately, visibility hidden
   814→  after 250ms). `isCompact` computed as `viewType === 'root' && !query.trim()`.
   815→  Collapsible wrapper wraps body + ActionPanel; overlays (QuickLook, Alert,
   816→  Toast, HUD) remain outside. Accessibility: `aria-hidden` and `inert` on
   817→  collapsible wrapper when compact to prevent focus/screen-reader access to
   818→  hidden content. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   819→
   820→- [ ] [P3] THEME003 - Add proper semantic color system. Create Color enum/object
   821→  matching Raycast's: Blue, Green, Magenta, Orange, Purple, Red, Yellow,
   822→  PrimaryText, SecondaryText. Each color auto-adapts to current theme. Use
   823→  CSS custom properties. Replace hardcoded hex in tag_colors.ts with semantic
   824→  colors. Create src/constants/colors.ts.
   825→
   826→=== KEYBOARD & INTERACTION ===
   827→
   828→- [x] [P1] KBD001 - Implement global keyboard shortcut system. Create
   829→  src/hooks/use_keyboard_shortcuts.ts that manages all global shortcuts in one
   830→  place. Support modifier combos (⌘, ⌥, ⌃, ⇧). Prevent conflicts. Register
   831→  shortcuts declaratively from components. Replace ad-hoc keydown handlers in
   832→  App.tsx with centralized system.
   833→  Done: Created src/hooks/use_keyboard_shortcut.ts with useKeyboardShortcut
   834→  hook. Declarative API: useKeyboardShortcut(combo, handler, options?). KeyCombo
   835→  supports meta/ctrl/alt/shift modifiers with exact matching. Module-level
   836→  registry with dev-mode warnings for duplicate combos and bare-key shortcuts
   837→  (no modifiers). Ref-based handler for stable closures. Enabled/preventDefault
   838→  options. Handler receives KeyboardEvent for flexibility. Refactored App.tsx:
   839→  replaced ad-hoc useEffect+addEventListener handlers for ⌘K (toggle actions),
   840→  ⌘N (create snippet), and ⌘Enter (form submit) with useKeyboardShortcut calls.
   841→  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   842→
   843→- [x] [P2] KBD002 - Add favorite items support. ⌘⇧F toggles favorite on
   844→  selected item. Favorited items appear in a "Favorites" section at top of
   845→  root search. Persist favorites in state (localStorage for web demo). Show
   846→  star icon on favorited items. ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorders favorites.
   847→  Done: useFavorites hook (src/hooks/use_favorites.ts) with localStorage
   848→  persistence, Set-based O(1) isFavorite lookup, toggleFavorite, moveFavorite
   849→  (swap adjacent). StarFilledIcon added to icon system. App.tsx integration:
   850→  Favorites section at top of root search (items deduplicated from original
   851→  sections), ⌘⇧F toggle with HUD feedback, ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorder with selectedIndex
   852→  tracking, "Add/Remove from Favorites" action in dropdown. Star accessory
   853→  shown only outside Favorites section (avoids redundancy with section title).
   854→  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   855→
   856→- [x] [P2] KBD003 - Add ⌃N/⌃P alternative navigation. In addition to arrow
   857→  keys, support Ctrl+N (down) and Ctrl+P (up) for Emacs-style navigation in
   858→  lists. Update list keyboard handler.
   859→  Done: Added Ctrl+N (next/down) and Ctrl+P (previous/up) Emacs-style navigation
   860→  to List and Grid components. Ctrl+N/P handled before the modifier guard with
   861→  exact same navigation logic as ArrowDown/ArrowUp. Grid uses 2D row-based
   862→  vertical movement matching ArrowDown/ArrowUp behavior. Guard: ctrlKey only
   863→  (no meta, no alt). Shift+Ctrl excluded naturally via lowercase key check.
   864→  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
   865→
   866→=== INFRASTRUCTURE ===
   867→
   868→- [x] [P2] INFRA001 - Create proper extension/command registry system. Instead
   869→  of hardcoded mock data, create a registry where "extensions" register
   870→  commands with: name, icon, keywords, component (for push), aliases. Root
   871→  search queries this registry. Create src/registry/command_registry.ts and
   872→  src/registry/types.ts. Refactor mock_data.ts to register through this
   873→  system.
   874→  Done: CommandRegistration interface in types.ts. command_registry.ts with
   875→  Map-based register/get/getSections/search (preserves section order, searches
   876→  title/subtitle/keywords/aliases). All 15 commands registered in commands.ts.
   877→  Barrel export in index.ts with side-effect import. App.tsx refactored:
   878→  NavViewData simplified to 'command'|'detail', handleDrillIn uses getCommand()
   879→  lookup, isFullView derived from registry. Dead filterSections function removed.
   880→  MOCK_COLORS extracted to src/data/color_data.ts, mock_data.ts deleted.
   881→  ColorPickerView updated to import from color_data.ts. All 93 tests pass.
   882→  Sub-tasks:
   883→  - [x] INFRA001a - Create registry types (src/registry/types.ts) with
   884→    CommandRegistration interface and registry module (src/registry/command_registry.ts)
   885→    with register/get/getSections/search functions.
   886→  - [x] INFRA001b - Extract ColorPickerView to standalone self-contained
   887→    component (like ClipboardHistoryView), register ALL existing commands
   888→    through the registry (src/registry/commands.ts).
   889→  - [x] INFRA001c - Refactor App.tsx to consume registry: replace hardcoded
   890→    handleDrillIn switch with registry lookup, simplify NavViewData type,
   891→    replace MOCK_SECTIONS import with registry.getSections(), derive isFullView
   892→    from registry.
   893→
   894→- [x] [P2] INFRA002 - Add localStorage persistence layer. Save and restore:
   895→  recent commands, favorites, clipboard history, snippets, settings. Create
   896→  src/utils/storage.ts with typed get/set helpers. Used by screens that need
   897→  state persistence.
   898→  Done: Created src/utils/storage.ts with storageGet/storageSet/storageRemove,
   899→  JSON serialization, try-catch, and runtime validators (isStringArray, isString,
   900→  isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Refactored use_favorites.ts, use_theme.ts, and
   901→  emoji_picker_view.tsx to use storage.ts with legacy migration for existing
   902→  localStorage keys. Settings persistence: GeneralSettings + extension states
   903→  via isBooleanRecord shared validator, appearance synced with useTheme hook.
   904→  Clipboard persistence: pin states + deleted entry IDs tracked separately.
   905→  Snippet persistence: full CRUD with StoredSnippet serialization (Date→ISO),
   906→  deserialization with Invalid Date guard, isStoredTag deep validation,
   907→  crypto.randomUUID() for collision-free IDs. All consumers use "raycast-"
   908→  prefixed keys via storage.ts. Zero raw localStorage calls outside migration paths.
   909→  Sub-tasks:
   910→  - [x] INFRA002a - Create src/utils/storage.ts with typed get/set/remove helpers.
   911→    Generic storage functions with JSON serialization, try-catch error handling,
   912→    and runtime validation via caller-provided validator function.
   913→  - [x] INFRA002b - Refactor existing localStorage usages to use storage.ts:
   914→    use_favorites.ts, use_theme.ts, emoji_picker_view.tsx.
   915→  - [x] INFRA002c - Add persistence for settings (GeneralSettings + extensions
   916→    enabled/disabled state) in SettingsView.
   917→  - [x] INFRA002d - Add persistence for clipboard history entries (pinned state,
   918→    entry mutations) in ClipboardHistoryView.
   919→  - [x] INFRA002e - Add persistence for snippet entries (CRUD operations) in
   920→    SnippetManagerView.
   921→
   922→- [ ] [P3] INFRA003 - Refactor App.tsx into smaller modules. Current App.tsx is
   923→  660+ lines. Extract: RootSearchView, NavigationProvider, KeyboardProvider,
   924→  command handlers. Each view should be its own component file. App.tsx should
   925→  only compose providers and the root view.
   926→
   927→=== TESTS ===
   928→
   929→- [ ] [P1000] TEST001 - Add visual regression tests with Playwright for all
   930→  new components (Alert, HUD, navigation transitions, List.Dropdown,
   931→  Form.TagPicker, Form.PasswordField). Each test captures screenshot and
   932→  compares. Create tests/ directory with playwright config.
   933→
   934→- [ ] [P1000] TEST002 - Add interaction tests for navigation stack: push view,
   935→  pop with Escape, popToRoot with ⌘Escape, breadcrumb updates. Test with
   936→  Playwright.
   937→
   938→- [ ] [P1000] TEST003 - Add interaction tests for ActionPanel.Section and
   939→  ActionPanel.Submenu: open dropdown, navigate sections, enter submenu,
   940→  escape back to parent. Test with Playwright.
   941→
   942→- [ ] [P1000] TEST004 - Add unit tests for new hooks: useAlert, useHUD,
   943→  useNavigation, useKeyboardShortcuts. Test with vitest.
   944→
   945→- [ ] [P1000] TEST005 - Add visual tests for Clipboard History, Snippet
   946→  Manager, Emoji Picker, Calculator, File Search screens. Capture screenshots
   947→  at various states (empty, loaded, searching, selecting).
   948→
   949→- [ ] [P1000] TEST006 - Add visual tests for light theme. All existing
   950→  component screenshots re-captured in light mode. Compare with dark mode
   951→  baselines to ensure proper contrast.
   952→

- [x] UXDS003 - Map all real Raycast screens and components, plan every single
one, add tasks.
  Mapped all Raycast screens/components from official API docs, manual, and
  design artifacts. Compared with existing implementation. Identified ~40 missing
  components/screens. Added tasks below organized by priority.

- [IN_PROGRESS] T0001 - Plan and add minimum 50 tasks to implement real Tauri Raycast
clone app.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RAYCAST CLONE — REMAINING UI/UX TASKS (from UXDS003 mapping)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

=== CORE MISSING COMPONENTS ===

- [x] [P0] COMP001 - Build Alert/Confirm dialog component. Blocking modal overlay
  for destructive or important confirmations. Fields: title, message, icon,
  primaryAction (label + style: Default/Destructive/Cancel), dismissAction.
  Animate in with backdrop blur + scale. Create src/components/alert/ with
  alert.tsx and alert.scss. Create useAlert hook in src/hooks/use_alert.ts that
  returns confirmAlert(options) -> Promise<boolean>. Wire demo: add "Delete"
  action to list items that triggers confirm dialog before toast.
  Done: Alert component with backdrop blur overlay, scale+fade enter/exit
  animations, focus trap (Tab cycling), Escape to dismiss, backdrop click to
  dismiss. useAlert hook with Promise-based confirmAlert() using ref-based
  resolve (no double-resolve). All SCSS values tokenized ($alert-dialog-width,
  $backdrop-blur, $backdrop-overlay added to _tokens.scss). Unique IDs via
  useId(). Stable callback refs to avoid stale closures. Delete action in
  dropdown triggers destructive confirm dialog before showing toast.

- [x] [P0] COMP002 - Build proper navigation stack system (push/pop). Replace
  current breadcrumb-only drill-in with a real stack-based navigation. Create
  src/hooks/use_navigation.ts with push(component), pop(), popToRoot(). Each
  pushed view is a full-screen replacement inside CommandPalette. Escape pops
  the stack. Breadcrumb updates automatically from stack. Animate transitions
  (slide-left on push, slide-right on pop). Update App.tsx to use navigation
  stack instead of viewMode state. Action.Push should be declarative.
  Done: useNavigationStack hook with push/pop/popToRoot, monotonic counter for
  stable animation keys, functional updater guards for race-safe pop/popToRoot,
  shared BreadcrumbItem type between hook and SearchBar, extracted ColorPickerView
  and DetailView as standalone components, CSS slide transitions in
  command_palette.scss, SearchBar Escape-to-pop with query-clear-first behavior.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] COMP002a - Create use_navigation hook (src/hooks/use_navigation.ts) with
    NavigationStack type, push(title, component), pop(), popToRoot(). Derive
    breadcrumbs automatically from stack. Export NavigationProvider context.
  - [x] COMP002b - Add navigation transition styles to command_palette.scss.
    Slide-left on push, slide-right on pop. CSS-only transitions.
  - [x] COMP002c - Refactor App.tsx to use navigation stack. Replace viewMode/
    drilledItem/breadcrumbs state with useNavigation hook. Extract ColorPickerView
    and CreateSnippetView as standalone pushed components. Root view stays inline.
  - [x] COMP002d - Update SearchBar to consume breadcrumbs from navigation
    context (or keep prop-based) and wire Escape to pop().

- [x] [P0] COMP003 - Add ActionPanel.Section support. Group actions in the
  dropdown with visual section separators and optional section titles. Update
  actions_dropdown.tsx to accept sections (array of {title?, actions[]}). Render
  separator line + small title between groups. Update App.tsx demo to show
  sections: "Primary Actions", "Copy Actions", "Danger Zone".
  Done: DropdownSection type with optional title. Sections prop replaces flat
  actions. Separators between groups (outside role="group"). Empty sections
  filtered. aria-activedescendant on menu, aria-labelledby on titled groups.
  Precomputed sectionOffsets. Index-based keys. App.tsx demo with 3 sections.
  26 dropdown tests (5 new), all 74 tests pass.

- [x] [P0] COMP004 - Add ActionPanel.Submenu support. Nested submenu that
  replaces the dropdown content with child actions when hovered/selected. Show
  chevron-right indicator on parent item. Escape goes back to parent menu.
  Animate sideways transition. Update actions_dropdown.tsx.
  Done: DropdownAction.submenu field with DropdownSection[]. SubmenuState tracks
  parentActiveIndex for restore on exit. ChevronRight indicator on submenu items.
  Back button with role="menuitem" and aria-label. Slide-left/slide-right CSS
  animations. ArrowRight/Enter to enter, ArrowLeft/Escape to go back (restores
  parent position). App.tsx demo with "Copy As..." submenu (3 child actions).
  45 dropdown tests (5 new for submenu), all 93 tests pass.

- [x] [P0] COMP005 - Build List.Dropdown component (search bar filter). A small
  dropdown selector inside the SearchBar (right side) for secondary filtering
  (e.g., filter by type: All, Commands, Applications). Create
  src/components/search_bar/search_dropdown.tsx and search_dropdown.scss.
  Dropdown appears below search bar on click, styled like actions_dropdown.
  Supports sections. Update SearchBar to accept optional dropdown prop.
  Done: SearchDropdown component with sections support, keyboard navigation
  (ArrowUp/Down, Enter, Escape, Tab closes), click-outside dismiss, check
  icon for selected option, chevron rotation animation. Proper ARIA: listbox
  role, option role on divs, aria-selected, aria-activedescendant, useId()
  for unique IDs, aria-label on trigger. All values tokenized
  ($search-dropdown-trigger-height, $search-dropdown-menu-min-width,
  $search-dropdown-menu-max-height, $z-dropdown, $z-search-dropdown).
  SearchBar accepts optional dropdown ReactNode prop. App.tsx demo with
  All/Commands/Applications filter. 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P1] COMP006 - Build HUD notification component. Compact floating pill
  shown at bottom-center of screen after an action completes (like "Copied to
  clipboard"). Auto-dismisses after 1.5s. Simpler than toast: just icon + text.
  Animated entrance (scale-up + fade). Create src/components/hud/ with hud.tsx
  and hud.scss. Create useHUD hook in src/hooks/use_hud.ts. Wire demo: "Copy"
  action shows HUD.
  Done: HUD component with pill shape ($radius-pill token), scale+fade
  enter/exit animations, role="status" aria-live="polite". useHUD hook follows
  useToast pattern (timer management, exit animation, auto-dismiss 1.5s).
  HUDContainer absolute-positioned at bottom-center with pointer-events:none.
  $z-hud: 120 token. Copy Name, Copy As... submenu, and grid color copy actions
  all use HUD instead of toast. CheckIcon and ClipboardHUDIcon inline SVGs.
  max-width: 280px with text-overflow ellipsis. All 93 tests pass.

- [x] [P1] COMP007 - Build Form.PasswordField component. Masked text input with
  optional reveal toggle button (eye icon). Same styling as FormTextField but
  type="password". Create src/components/form/form_password_field.tsx. Add to
  form index.ts exports.
  Done: FormPasswordField component with password/text toggle via eye icon button.
  Follows FormTextField pattern exactly (useId, FormField wrapper, aria-invalid,

  - [x] INFRA002c - Add persistence for settings (GeneralSettings + extensions
    enabled/disabled state) in SettingsView.
  - [x] INFRA002d - Add persistence for clipboard history entries (pinned state,
    entry mutations) in ClipboardHistoryView.
  - [x] INFRA002e - Add persistence for snippet entries (CRUD operations) in
    SnippetManagerView.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA003 - Refactor App.tsx into smaller modules. Current App.tsx is
  660+ lines. Extract: RootSearchView, NavigationProvider, KeyboardProvider,
  command handlers. Each view should be its own component file. App.tsx should
  only compose providers and the root view.

=== TESTS ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST001 - Add visual regression tests with Playwright for all
  new components (Alert, HUD, navigation transitions, List.Dropdown,
  Form.TagPicker, Form.PasswordField). Each test captures screenshot and
  compares. Create tests/ directory with playwright config.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST002 - Add interaction tests for navigation stack: push view,
  pop with Escape, popToRoot with ⌘Escape, breadcrumb updates. Test with
  Playwright.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST003 - Add interaction tests for ActionPanel.Section and
  ActionPanel.Submenu: open dropdown, navigate sections, enter submenu,
  escape back to parent. Test with Playwright.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST004 - Add unit tests for new hooks: useAlert, useHUD,
  useNavigation, useKeyboardShortcuts. Test with vitest.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST005 - Add visual tests for Clipboard History, Snippet
  Manager, Emoji Picker, Calculator, File Search screens. Capture screenshots
  at various states (empty, loaded, searching, selecting).

- [ ] [P1000] TEST006 - Add visual tests for light theme. All existing
  component screenshots re-captured in light mode. Compare with dark mode
  baselines to ensure proper contrast.

RAYCAST CLONE — REMAINING UI/UX TASKS (from UXDS003 mapping)

- [x] [P0] COMP001 - Build Alert/Confirm dialog component. Blocking modal overlay

  alert.tsx and alert.scss. Create useAlert hook in src/hooks/use_alert.ts that

  action to list items that triggers confirm dialog before toast.
  Done: Alert component with backdrop blur overlay, scale+fade enter/exit
  animations, focus trap (Tab cycling), Escape to dismiss, backdrop click to
  dismiss. useAlert hook with Promise-based confirmAlert() using ref-based
  resolve (no double-resolve). All SCSS values tokenized ($alert-dialog-width,
  $backdrop-blur, $backdrop-overlay added to _tokens.scss). Unique IDs via
  useId(). Stable callback refs to avoid stale closures. Delete action in
  dropdown triggers destructive confirm dialog before showing toast.

- [x] [P0] COMP002 - Build proper navigation stack system (push/pop). Replace
  current breadcrumb-only drill-in with a real stack-based navigation. Create
  src/hooks/use_navigation.ts with push(component), pop(), popToRoot(). Each
  pushed view is a full-screen replacement inside CommandPalette. Escape pops
  the stack. Breadcrumb updates automatically from stack. Animate transitions
  (slide-left on push, slide-right on pop). Update App.tsx to use navigation
