  (no modifiers). Ref-based handler for stable closures. Enabled/preventDefault
  options. Handler receives KeyboardEvent for flexibility. Refactored App.tsx:
  replaced ad-hoc useEffect+addEventListener handlers for ⌘K (toggle actions),
  ⌘N (create snippet), and ⌘Enter (form submit) with useKeyboardShortcut calls.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P2] KBD002 - Add favorite items support. ⌘⇧F toggles favorite on
  selected item. Favorited items appear in a "Favorites" section at top of
  root search. Persist favorites in state (localStorage for web demo). Show
  star icon on favorited items. ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorders favorites.
  Done: useFavorites hook (src/hooks/use_favorites.ts) with localStorage
  persistence, Set-based O(1) isFavorite lookup, toggleFavorite, moveFavorite
  (swap adjacent). StarFilledIcon added to icon system. App.tsx integration:
  Favorites section at top of root search (items deduplicated from original
  sections), ⌘⇧F toggle with HUD feedback, ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorder with selectedIndex
  tracking, "Add/Remove from Favorites" action in dropdown. Star accessory
  shown only outside Favorites section (avoids redundancy with section title).
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P2] KBD003 - Add ⌃N/⌃P alternative navigation. In addition to arrow
  keys, support Ctrl+N (down) and Ctrl+P (up) for Emacs-style navigation in
  lists. Update list keyboard handler.
  Done: Added Ctrl+N (next/down) and Ctrl+P (previous/up) Emacs-style navigation
  to List and Grid components. Ctrl+N/P handled before the modifier guard with
  exact same navigation logic as ArrowDown/ArrowUp. Grid uses 2D row-based
  vertical movement matching ArrowDown/ArrowUp behavior. Guard: ctrlKey only
  (no meta, no alt). Shift+Ctrl excluded naturally via lowercase key check.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

=== INFRASTRUCTURE ===

- [x] [P2] INFRA001 - Create proper extension/command registry system. Instead
  of hardcoded mock data, create a registry where "extensions" register
  commands with: name, icon, keywords, component (for push), aliases. Root
  search queries this registry. Create src/registry/command_registry.ts and
  src/registry/types.ts. Refactor mock_data.ts to register through this
  system.
  Done: CommandRegistration interface in types.ts. command_registry.ts with
  Map-based register/get/getSections/search (preserves section order, searches
  title/subtitle/keywords/aliases). All 15 commands registered in commands.ts.
  Barrel export in index.ts with side-effect import. App.tsx refactored:
  NavViewData simplified to 'command'|'detail', handleDrillIn uses getCommand()
  lookup, isFullView derived from registry. Dead filterSections function removed.
  MOCK_COLORS extracted to src/data/color_data.ts, mock_data.ts deleted.
  ColorPickerView updated to import from color_data.ts. All 93 tests pass.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] INFRA001a - Create registry types (src/registry/types.ts) with
    CommandRegistration interface and registry module (src/registry/command_registry.ts)
    with register/get/getSections/search functions.
  - [x] INFRA001b - Extract ColorPickerView to standalone self-contained
    component (like ClipboardHistoryView), register ALL existing commands
    through the registry (src/registry/commands.ts).
  - [x] INFRA001c - Refactor App.tsx to consume registry: replace hardcoded
    handleDrillIn switch with registry lookup, simplify NavViewData type,
    replace MOCK_SECTIONS import with registry.getSections(), derive isFullView
    from registry.

- [x] [P2] INFRA002 - Add localStorage persistence layer. Save and restore:
  recent commands, favorites, clipboard history, snippets, settings. Create
  src/utils/storage.ts with typed get/set helpers. Used by screens that need
  state persistence.
  Done: Created src/utils/storage.ts with storageGet/storageSet/storageRemove,
  JSON serialization, try-catch, and runtime validators (isStringArray, isString,
  isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Refactored use_favorites.ts, use_theme.ts, and
  emoji_picker_view.tsx to use storage.ts with legacy migration for existing
  localStorage keys. Settings persistence: GeneralSettings + extension states
  via isBooleanRecord shared validator, appearance synced with useTheme hook.
  Clipboard persistence: pin states + deleted entry IDs tracked separately.
  Snippet persistence: full CRUD with StoredSnippet serialization (Date→ISO),
  deserialization with Invalid Date guard, isStoredTag deep validation,
  crypto.randomUUID() for collision-free IDs. All consumers use "raycast-"
  prefixed keys via storage.ts. Zero raw localStorage calls outside migration paths.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] INFRA002a - Create src/utils/storage.ts with typed get/set/remove helpers.
    Generic storage functions with JSON serialization, try-catch error handling,
    and runtime validation via caller-provided validator function.
  - [x] INFRA002b - Refactor existing localStorage usages to use storage.ts:
    use_favorites.ts, use_theme.ts, emoji_picker_view.tsx.
  - [x] INFRA002c - Add persistence for settings (GeneralSettings + extensions
    enabled/disabled state) in SettingsView.
  - [x] INFRA002d - Add persistence for clipboard history entries (pinned state,
    entry mutations) in ClipboardHistoryView.
  - [x] INFRA002e - Add persistence for snippet entries (CRUD operations) in
    SnippetManagerView.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA003 - Refactor App.tsx into smaller modules. Current App.tsx is
  660+ lines. Extract: RootSearchView, NavigationProvider, KeyboardProvider,
  command handlers. Each view should be its own component file. App.tsx should
  only compose providers and the root view.

=== TESTS ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST001 - Add visual regression tests with Playwright for all
  new components (Alert, HUD, navigation transitions, List.Dropdown,
  Form.TagPicker, Form.PasswordField). Each test captures screenshot and
  compares. Create tests/ directory with playwright config.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST002 - Add interaction tests for navigation stack: push view,
  pop with Escape, popToRoot with ⌘Escape, breadcrumb updates. Test with
  Playwright.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST003 - Add interaction tests for ActionPanel.Section and
  ActionPanel.Submenu: open dropdown, navigate sections, enter submenu,
  escape back to parent. Test with Playwright.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST004 - Add unit tests for new hooks: useAlert, useHUD,
  useNavigation, useKeyboardShortcuts. Test with vitest.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST005 - Add visual tests for Clipboard History, Snippet
  Manager, Emoji Picker, Calculator, File Search screens. Capture screenshots
  at various states (empty, loaded, searching, selecting).

- [ ] [P1000] TEST006 - Add visual tests for light theme. All existing
  component screenshots re-captured in light mode. Compare with dark mode
  baselines to ensure proper contrast.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST006 - Add visual tests for light theme. All existing
  component screenshots re-captured in light mode. Compare with dark mode

================================================================================
TAURI DESKTOP APP — NEW TASKS (from T0001 planning)
================================================================================

=== TAURI DESKTOP INTEGRATION ===

- [ ] [P0] TAURI001 - Initialize Tauri v2 project structure. Run `bun add -D
  @tauri-apps/cli@next` and `bunx tauri init`. Configure src-tauri/ with
  Cargo.toml, tauri.conf.json (identifier, window settings, bundle config).
  Set up frameless window (decorations: false) with transparent background.
  Window size: 750x500px, centered, always-on-top option. Configure
  allowlist for needed Tauri APIs. Verify `bun run tauri dev` launches the
  app with the existing React frontend. Update package.json scripts:
  "tauri:dev", "tauri:build".

- [ ] [P0] TAURI002 - Implement global hotkey to toggle window visibility.
  Use @tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut to register Option+Space (macOS)
  / Alt+Space (Windows) as global hotkey. Toggle window show/hide on
  keypress. When showing: center window on active monitor, focus search
  input. When hiding: clear search query, pop navigation to root. Create
  src-tauri/src/lib.rs commands for show/hide. Handle edge case: if window
  is visible but unfocused, bring to front instead of hiding.

- [ ] [P0] TAURI003 - Add system tray with menu. Use @tauri-apps/api/tray
  to create system tray icon (Raycast logo). Tray menu items: Show Raycast
  (trigger global hotkey), Clipboard History, Snippets, Settings, separator,
  Check for Updates, About, Quit. Clicking tray icon toggles window. Create
  tray icon SVG (16x16 and 32x32 for retina). Wire tray menu actions to
  IPC commands that push appropriate views.

- [ ] [P0] TAURI004 - Configure window behavior to match Raycast. Hide on
  blur (window loses focus → hide, like Spotlight). Disable window
  resize/maximize. Position window: top-third of screen vertically, centered
  horizontally. Persist window position preference in settings. Add
  vibrancy/blur effect on macOS (NSVisualEffectView via Tauri window
  effects). Handle multi-monitor: show on monitor where mouse cursor is.
  Create src/hooks/use_window.ts for window management from React side.

- [ ] [P1] TAURI005 - Add native clipboard access via Tauri plugin. Use
  @tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager for real clipboard read/write.
  Replace mock clipboard data with actual system clipboard monitoring.
  Create src-tauri/ Rust command to poll clipboard changes (500ms interval).
  Emit clipboard-changed event to frontend via Tauri event system. Store
  clipboard history in SQLite (TAURI008). Detect content type (text, image,
  link, color hex). Limit history to 1000 entries. Wire into
  ClipboardHistoryView to replace mock data.

- [ ] [P1] TAURI006 - Add native file system access. Use @tauri-apps/api/fs
  and @tauri-apps/plugin-fs for real file operations. Replace mock file
  search with real directory scanning. Create Rust command for fast
  recursive file search (ignore node_modules, .git, hidden dirs by default).
  Index common directories (~/, ~/Documents, ~/Downloads, ~/Desktop).
  Return file metadata (size, modified date, type). Wire into FileSearchView
  to replace mock data. Add "Open" action that uses @tauri-apps/api/shell
  to open files with default app.

- [ ] [P1] TAURI007 - Add native app launching. Use @tauri-apps/api/shell
  to launch applications. On macOS: scan /Applications and ~/Applications
  for .app bundles, extract app name and icon. On Windows: scan Start Menu
  shortcuts. Create Rust command for app discovery that returns name, path,
  icon (base64 PNG). Cache app list and refresh on focus. Wire into root
  search: show matching apps in "Applications" section. Enter launches app
  and hides window.

- [ ] [P2] TAURI008 - Add SQLite persistence layer via Tauri. Use
  @tauri-apps/plugin-sql with SQLite. Create database schema: clipboard_
  entries, snippets, quicklinks, notes, recent_commands, settings tables.
  Migrate from localStorage to SQLite for all persistence (use localStorage
  as web fallback when Tauri is not available). Create src/utils/database.ts
  with typed query helpers. Rust-side migrations for schema versioning.
  Full-text search index on text content for fast querying.

- [ ] [P2] TAURI009 - Configure auto-launch on login. Use
  @tauri-apps/plugin-autostart to register app for login auto-start.
  Add toggle in Settings > General: "Launch at Login" checkbox. Default:
  enabled. Store preference in settings. Handle platform differences
  (macOS LaunchAgent, Windows registry, Linux autostart desktop file).

- [ ] [P2] TAURI010 - Add auto-update system. Use @tauri-apps/plugin-updater
  for in-app updates. Configure update endpoint URL in tauri.conf.json.
  Check for updates on launch (silent) and via Settings > Advanced. Show
  toast notification when update available. "Install Update & Restart"
  action. Progress bar during download. Changelog display before install.
  Sign updates with Tauri's built-in code signing.

=== MISSING RAYCAST SCREENS ===

- [ ] [P1] SCRN010 - Build Quicklinks screen. List view of saved quicklinks
  (URLs, file paths, deep links). Each item: icon (auto-detected from URL
  favicon or file type), name, URL/path as subtitle. Actions: Open (⏎),
  Edit (⌘E pushes form), Copy Link (⌘C), Delete (⌘⌫). Create quicklink
  form: name, link (URL/path/deep link), icon selection, application to
  open with. Support query placeholders in URLs (e.g. "https://google.com/
  search?q={query}" — when opened, prompts for query input). Push from
  "Search Quicklinks" command. Create src/data/quicklinks_data.ts with 10
  mock quicklinks and src/components/quicklinks/quicklinks_view.tsx.

- [ ] [P1] SCRN011 - Build Window Management screen. Grid or list of window
  layout presets: Left Half, Right Half, Top Half, Bottom Half, Center,
  Maximize, First Third, Middle Third, Last Third, First Two Thirds, Last
  Two Thirds, Top Left Quarter, Top Right Quarter, Bottom Left Quarter,
  Bottom Right Quarter. Each preset shows a visual diagram (CSS box showing
  the layout position highlighted). Keyboard shortcuts for each position
  (configurable). In Tauri mode, use @tauri-apps/api/window to actually
  resize/reposition windows. In web mode, show visual demo only. Create
  src/components/window_management/window_management_view.tsx and
  src/data/window_layouts_data.ts.

- [ ] [P2] SCRN012 - Build Floating Notes screen. Markdown-enabled note
  editor that floats above other apps. In-memory list of notes with titles.
  Notes list view with search. Click note to open editor. Editor: simple
  textarea with markdown preview toggle. Auto-save on change (debounced
  500ms). Create new note via ⌘N. Delete note with confirm. Persist notes
  via localStorage/SQLite. Push from "Floating Notes" command. Create
  src/components/floating_notes/floating_notes_view.tsx and
  floating_notes_editor.tsx.

- [ ] [P2] SCRN013 - Build Translator screen. Two-panel layout: source text
  (left/top) and translated text (right/bottom). Language selector dropdowns
  for source and target. Swap languages button. Auto-detect source language.
  Copy translation button. In web demo mode, use a simple mock translation
  (reverse text or placeholder). In production, integrate with translation
  API. Support 20+ languages. Create src/components/translator/
  translator_view.tsx and translator.scss. Push from "Translate" command.

- [ ] [P2] SCRN014 - Build Reminders screen. List of reminders with
  title, due date/time, and repeat options. Create reminder form: title,
  date (FormDatePicker), time, repeat (none/daily/weekly/monthly), notes.
  Sections: Overdue, Today, Upcoming, Completed. Toggle complete with
  checkbox. Delete with confirm. In Tauri mode, use OS notification API
  for due reminders. Persist via localStorage/SQLite. Push from "Reminders"
  command. Create src/components/reminders/reminders_view.tsx and
  src/data/reminders_data.ts.

- [ ] [P3] SCRN015 - Build Script Commands screen. List view of user-defined
  script commands. Each item: name, description, language icon (bash, python,
  node, ruby), last run date. Actions: Run (⏎) shows output in detail panel,
  Edit (opens in editor), Copy Script, Delete. Mock script execution with
  simulated output (typewriter effect). In Tauri mode, use
  @tauri-apps/api/shell to actually execute scripts. Create
  src/components/script_commands/script_commands_view.tsx with mock scripts.

=== MISSING UI COMPONENTS ===

- [ ] [P1] COMP016 - Build Action.CopyToClipboard declarative action. Create
  reusable action component that copies content to clipboard and shows HUD
  "Copied to Clipboard". Accepts: content (string), title (default "Copy"),
  shortcut. Uses navigator.clipboard.writeText in web, Tauri clipboard
  plugin in desktop. Create src/components/action_panel/actions/ directory
  with copy_to_clipboard.tsx. Replace ad-hoc clipboard.writeText calls
  across all views (ClipboardHistory, SnippetManager, EmojiPicker,
  Calculator, FileSearch, App.tsx) with this reusable action.

- [ ] [P1] COMP017 - Build Action.OpenInBrowser declarative action. Opens
  URL in default browser. Accepts: url, title (default "Open in Browser"),
  shortcut. In web: window.open(url). In Tauri: @tauri-apps/api/shell open.
  Shows HUD "Opened in Browser". Create open_in_browser.tsx in actions/.

- [ ] [P1] COMP018 - Build List.Item.Detail inline detail panel. When a
  ListItem has a detail prop, the list splits into list (left, ~40%) and
  detail panel (right, ~60%) showing the item's detail content. Reuses
  Detail component. Toggle detail panel visibility with an action or
  automatic on selection. Animate panel slide-in. Update List component
  to accept showDetail prop. Create list_item_detail.scss for split layout.

- [ ] [P2] COMP019 - Build Action.PickDate component. Date picker that
  appears inline in action panel or as a pushed form field. Supports
  date-only and date+time modes. Min/max date constraints. Calendar grid
  UI with month/year navigation. Selected date highlighted with accent.
  Keyboard navigation: arrows for days, PageUp/Down for months. Create
  src/components/date_picker/ with date_picker.tsx and date_picker.scss.

- [ ] [P2] COMP020 - Add List pagination support (infinite scroll). Add
  onLoadMore callback and hasMore boolean props to List. When user scrolls
  near bottom (IntersectionObserver on sentinel element), trigger
  onLoadMore. Show loading skeleton items (3 placeholder rows with pulse
  animation) while loading. Update List component. Demo: Clipboard History
  loads 20 items at a time.

- [ ] [P2] COMP021 - Build markdown renderer for Detail component. Replace
  current simple text rendering with proper CommonMark markdown. Support:
  headings, bold, italic, code (inline + blocks with syntax highlighting),
  links, lists (ordered + unordered), blockquotes, tables, horizontal rules,
  images (as placeholders in web). No external markdown library — build
  minimal parser or use a small one (micromark ~4KB). Create
  src/utils/markdown.ts. Update Detail component to render parsed markdown.

- [ ] [P2] COMP022 - Build Form.LinkAccessory component. Small accessory
  displayed in the right side of the SearchBar/navigation bar when a Form
  is active. Shows a link with icon + label. Clickable, opens URL or
  triggers action. Create src/components/form/form_link_accessory.tsx.
  Update SearchBar to accept optional rightAccessory prop. Demo: "View Docs"
  link in Create Snippet form.

- [ ] [P3] COMP023 - Build color swatch accessory for ListItem. When a
  ListItem accessory has a color hex value, render a small color circle
  (12x12px, rounded) next to the text. Used in clipboard history for copied
  colors and in color picker. Update ListItem rendering to detect color
  accessory type and render swatch. Add color?: string field to
  ListItemAccessoryData.

- [ ] [P3] COMP024 - Build empty search fallback actions. When root search
  has no matching results, show fallback items: "Search Google for '{query}'",
  "Search DuckDuckGo for '{query}'", "Search Wikipedia for '{query}'". Each
  item has appropriate search engine icon. Enter opens search URL in browser.
  Configurable fallback list in settings. Create src/data/fallback_data.ts.
  Update App.tsx to show fallbacks when filteredSections is empty.

=== VISUAL POLISH ===

- [ ] [P1] VIS001 - Add virtual scrolling for large lists. Current List
  renders all items in DOM which becomes slow with 100+ items. Implement
  virtual scroll: only render visible items + buffer (5 items above/below
  viewport). Calculate item positions from fixed height ($list-item-height).
  Maintain scroll position on item count changes. Keep keyboard navigation
  working (scrollIntoView on active item). Create src/hooks/use_virtual_
  scroll.ts. Apply to List component. Test with 1000+ items in Clipboard
  History.

- [ ] [P2] VIS002 - Add custom window title bar. Since Tauri uses frameless
  window, build a custom title bar. macOS style: traffic light buttons
  (close/minimize/maximize) on left side, centered title text. Make it
  draggable (data-tauri-drag-region). In web mode, hide title bar (browser
  has its own). Create src/components/title_bar/ with title_bar.tsx and
  title_bar.scss. Position above CommandPalette. 28px height matching macOS.

- [ ] [P2] VIS003 - Add smooth scroll momentum and snap-to-item. Lists
  should have native-feeling scroll momentum (CSS scroll-behavior: smooth).
  When scroll stops, snap to nearest item boundary (CSS scroll-snap-type:
  y proximity on list container, scroll-snap-align: start on items). Ensure
  keyboard navigation scrollIntoView uses smooth behavior. Test on macOS
  trackpad and mouse wheel.

- [ ] [P2] VIS004 - Add focus ring animation matching Raycast. When
  navigating list items with keyboard, show a subtle animated focus ring
  that transitions between items (not just instant highlight switch).
  Use CSS transition on a pseudo-element that moves between item positions.
  The ring should have a very subtle glow (box-shadow with accent color at
  10% opacity). 80ms transition duration. Only show on keyboard navigation,
  not mouse hover.

- [ ] [P3] VIS005 - Add window open/close animations. When CommandPalette
  appears: macOS-style spring animation (scale from 0.95 to 1.0, opacity
  0 to 1, with slight overshoot). When closing: scale to 0.97, opacity to 0,
  faster (150ms). Use CSS @keyframes with cubic-bezier easing that mimics
  spring physics. In Tauri mode, coordinate with window show/hide timing.

- [ ] [P3] VIS006 - Add Grid view hover preview tooltip. When hovering a
  GridItem for 800ms, show an enlarged preview tooltip above the item
  (larger icon/image, full title, metadata). Positioned above the item,
  centered. Fade in/out. Dismiss on mouse leave. Create
  src/components/grid/grid_preview.tsx and grid_preview.scss.

- [ ] [P3] VIS007 - Add subtle parallax effect to command palette background.
  Very subtle background gradient shift based on mouse position (±2px max
  movement). Creates depth illusion matching Raycast's glass effect. CSS
  custom properties updated via mousemove listener (throttled to rAF). Only
  affects background layer, not content. Disable when prefers-reduced-motion.

=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===

- [ ] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  root search and all view search bars.

- [ ] [P1] SRCH002 - Add recent commands tracking. Track last 10 opened
  commands with timestamps. Show "Recent Commands" section in root search
  when query is empty (above other sections). Persist via localStorage/
  SQLite. When user opens a command (drill-in), add to recents. Deduplicate.
  Cap at 10. Create src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts. Wire into App.tsx
  root search sections.

- [ ] [P2] SRCH003 - Add search bar placeholder cycling. When search bar
  is empty, cycle through helpful placeholder texts every 4 seconds:
  "Type a command...", "Search applications...", "Calculate something...",
  "Find files...". Smooth fade transition between placeholders. Stop cycling
  when user starts typing. Resume on clear. Use CSS animation for opacity
  transitions. Update SearchBar component.

- [ ] [P2] SRCH004 - Add ⌘L to copy current item URL/value. Universal
  shortcut that copies the most relevant value of the selected item (URL
  for links, path for files, content for snippets, hex for colors). Show
  HUD with what was copied. Register via useKeyboardShortcut. Works across
  all views.

- [ ] [P2] SRCH005 - Add search debouncing and loading state. Debounce
  search input by 150ms to avoid excessive re-renders during fast typing.
  Show loading bar during debounce period. Create src/hooks/use_debounce.ts
  with generic useDebounce(value, delay) hook. Apply to all search bars.
  Cancel pending debounce on unmount.

- [ ] [P3] SRCH006 - Add Tab completion in search. When Tab is pressed in
  search and there's a single matching result, auto-complete the search
  query to match that result's title. If multiple matches share a common
  prefix, complete to that prefix. Visual: briefly highlight the completed
  portion. Works in root search only (not within views). Prevent Tab from
  focusing other elements during search.

=== INFRASTRUCTURE ===

- [ ] [P1] INFRA004 - Build extension architecture. Formalize each screen
  as an "extension" with a manifest. Create ExtensionManifest type in
  src/registry/types.ts: id, name, icon, description, author, commands[].
  Each command has: name, title, subtitle, keywords, mode (view/no-view/
  menu-bar), component. Refactor existing registry to use extension manifests.
  Create src/extensions/ directory with one folder per extension (clipboard/,
  snippets/, emoji/, etc). Each extension exports its manifest. Auto-discover
  extensions via barrel import.

- [ ] [P1] INFRA005 - Create Tauri IPC bridge layer. Abstract all
  platform-specific operations behind a unified interface. Create
  src/platform/index.ts with PlatformAPI interface: clipboard (read/write/
  monitor), files (search/open/trash), apps (list/launch), shell (execute),
  window (show/hide/position), notifications (send). Two implementations:
  src/platform/web.ts (browser fallbacks using navigator, localStorage, etc)
  and src/platform/tauri.ts (real native via @tauri-apps/api). Auto-detect
  environment and export correct implementation. All views import from
  platform/index.ts only.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA006 - Add error boundary components. Create
  src/components/error_boundary/ with error_boundary.tsx (class component
  wrapping React.ErrorBoundary). Show friendly error UI: icon, "Something
  went wrong" title, error message (collapsed by default, expandable),
  "Try Again" button that resets state. Style matching Raycast error states.
  Wrap each pushed view in error boundary. Log errors to console with
  component stack. Create error_boundary.scss.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA007 - Add state management with Zustand. Replace prop-
  drilling and scattered useState across App.tsx. Create stores:
  useAppStore (query, selectedIndex, actionsOpen, isFullView),
  useNavigationStore (stack, push, pop, breadcrumbs — replaces hook),
  useSettingsStore (general settings, extension states). Zustand selected
  for minimal API (no providers), TypeScript inference, devtools support,
  middleware (persist). Install zustand, create src/stores/ directory.
  Migrate App.tsx state to stores incrementally.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA008 - Add proper build configuration for production. Vite

- [ ] [P2] INFRA009 - Add CI/CD pipeline. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:

- [ ] [P3] INFRA011 - Add i18n internationalization support. Create

- [ ] [P3] INFRA012 - Add performance monitoring utilities. Create

=== TESTING (NEW) ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST007 - Add unit tests for calculator.ts. Test all math
  operations: basic arithmetic, operator precedence, parentheses, functions
  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,

- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove

  Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all

- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test

- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.

  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with

- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:

================================================================================
TAURI DESKTOP APP — NEW TASKS (from T0001 planning)
================================================================================

=== TAURI DESKTOP INTEGRATION ===

- [ ] [P0] TAURI001 - Initialize Tauri v2 project structure. Run `bun add -D
  @tauri-apps/cli@next` and `bunx tauri init`. Configure src-tauri/ with

  Set up frameless window (decorations: false) with transparent background.
  Window size: 750x500px, centered, always-on-top option. Configure
  allowlist for needed Tauri APIs. Verify `bun run tauri dev` launches the
  app with the existing React frontend. Update package.json scripts:
  "tauri:dev", "tauri:build".

- [ ] [P0] TAURI002 - Implement global hotkey to toggle window visibility.
  Use @tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut to register Option+Space (macOS)
  / Alt+Space (Windows) as global hotkey. Toggle window show/hide on
  keypress. When showing: center window on active monitor, focus search
  input. When hiding: clear search query, pop navigation to root. Create
  src-tauri/src/lib.rs commands for show/hide. Handle edge case: if window
  is visible but unfocused, bring to front instead of hiding.

- [ ] [P0] TAURI003 - Add system tray with menu. Use @tauri-apps/api/tray
  to create system tray icon (Raycast logo). Tray menu items: Show Raycast
  (trigger global hotkey), Clipboard History, Snippets, Settings, separator,
  Check for Updates, About, Quit. Clicking tray icon toggles window. Create
  tray icon SVG (16x16 and 32x32 for retina). Wire tray menu actions to
  IPC commands that push appropriate views.

- [ ] [P0] TAURI004 - Configure window behavior to match Raycast. Hide on
  blur (window loses focus → hide, like Spotlight). Disable window
  resize/maximize. Position window: top-third of screen vertically, centered
  horizontally. Persist window position preference in settings. Add
  vibrancy/blur effect on macOS (NSVisualEffectView via Tauri window
  effects). Handle multi-monitor: show on monitor where mouse cursor is.
  Create src/hooks/use_window.ts for window management from React side.

- [ ] [P1] TAURI005 - Add native clipboard access via Tauri plugin. Use
  @tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager for real clipboard read/write.
  Replace mock clipboard data with actual system clipboard monitoring.
  Create src-tauri/ Rust command to poll clipboard changes (500ms interval).
  Emit clipboard-changed event to frontend via Tauri event system. Store
  clipboard history in SQLite (TAURI008). Detect content type (text, image,
  link, color hex). Limit history to 1000 entries. Wire into
  ClipboardHistoryView to replace mock data.

- [ ] [P1] TAURI006 - Add native file system access. Use @tauri-apps/api/fs
  and @tauri-apps/plugin-fs for real file operations. Replace mock file
  search with real directory scanning. Create Rust command for fast
  recursive file search (ignore node_modules, .git, hidden dirs by default).
  Index common directories (~/, ~/Documents, ~/Downloads, ~/Desktop).
  Return file metadata (size, modified date, type). Wire into FileSearchView
  to replace mock data. Add "Open" action that uses @tauri-apps/api/shell
  to open files with default app.

- [ ] [P1] TAURI007 - Add native app launching. Use @tauri-apps/api/shell
  to launch applications. On macOS: scan /Applications and ~/Applications
  for .app bundles, extract app name and icon. On Windows: scan Start Menu
  shortcuts. Create Rust command for app discovery that returns name, path,
  icon (base64 PNG). Cache app list and refresh on focus. Wire into root
  search: show matching apps in "Applications" section. Enter launches app
  and hides window.

- [ ] [P2] TAURI008 - Add SQLite persistence layer via Tauri. Use
  @tauri-apps/plugin-sql with SQLite. Create database schema: clipboard_
  entries, snippets, quicklinks, notes, recent_commands, settings tables.
  Migrate from localStorage to SQLite for all persistence (use localStorage
  as web fallback when Tauri is not available). Create src/utils/database.ts
  with typed query helpers. Rust-side migrations for schema versioning.
  Full-text search index on text content for fast querying.

- [ ] [P2] TAURI009 - Configure auto-launch on login. Use
  @tauri-apps/plugin-autostart to register app for login auto-start.
  Add toggle in Settings > General: "Launch at Login" checkbox. Default:
  enabled. Store preference in settings. Handle platform differences
  (macOS LaunchAgent, Windows registry, Linux autostart desktop file).

- [ ] [P2] TAURI010 - Add auto-update system. Use @tauri-apps/plugin-updater
  for in-app updates. Configure update endpoint URL in tauri.conf.json.
  Check for updates on launch (silent) and via Settings > Advanced. Show
  toast notification when update available. "Install Update & Restart"
  action. Progress bar during download. Changelog display before install.
  Sign updates with Tauri's built-in code signing.

=== MISSING RAYCAST SCREENS ===

- [ ] [P1] SCRN010 - Build Quicklinks screen. List view of saved quicklinks

  form: name, link (URL/path/deep link), icon selection, application to

  search?q={query}" — when opened, prompts for query input). Push from

- [ ] [P1] SCRN011 - Build Window Management screen. Grid or list of window

- [ ] [P2] SCRN012 - Build Floating Notes screen. Markdown-enabled note

- [ ] [P2] SCRN013 - Build Translator screen. Two-panel layout: source text

  translator_view.tsx and translator.scss. Push from "Translate" command.

- [ ] [P2] SCRN014 - Build Reminders screen. List of reminders with

  checkbox. Delete with confirm. In Tauri mode, use OS notification API

  src/data/reminders_data.ts.

- [ ] [P3] SCRN015 - Build Script Commands screen. List view of user-defined
  script commands. Each item: name, description, language icon (bash, python,
  node, ruby), last run date. Actions: Run (⏎) shows output in detail panel,
  Edit (opens in editor), Copy Script, Delete. Mock script execution with
  simulated output (typewriter effect). In Tauri mode, use
  @tauri-apps/api/shell to actually execute scripts. Create
  src/components/script_commands/script_commands_view.tsx with mock scripts.

=== MISSING UI COMPONENTS ===

- [ ] [P1] COMP016 - Build Action.CopyToClipboard declarative action. Create

  across all views (ClipboardHistory, SnippetManager, EmojiPicker,
  Calculator, FileSearch, App.tsx) with this reusable action.

- [ ] [P1] COMP017 - Build Action.OpenInBrowser declarative action. Opens

- [ ] [P1] COMP018 - Build List.Item.Detail inline detail panel. When a
  ListItem has a detail prop, the list splits into list (left, ~40%) and

  Detail component. Toggle detail panel visibility with an action or

- [ ] [P2] COMP019 - Build Action.PickDate component. Date picker that

- [ ] [P2] COMP020 - Add List pagination support (infinite scroll). Add

- [ ] [P2] COMP021 - Build markdown renderer for Detail component. Replace
  current simple text rendering with proper CommonMark markdown. Support:

  images (as placeholders in web). No external markdown library — build
  minimal parser or use a small one (micromark ~4KB). Create
  src/utils/markdown.ts. Update Detail component to render parsed markdown.

- [ ] [P2] COMP022 - Build Form.LinkAccessory component. Small accessory
  displayed in the right side of the SearchBar/navigation bar when a Form

  triggers action. Create src/components/form/form_link_accessory.tsx.

- [ ] [P3] COMP023 - Build color swatch accessory for ListItem. When a

- [ ] [P3] COMP024 - Build empty search fallback actions. When root search
  has no matching results, show fallback items: "Search Google for '{query}'",

=== VISUAL POLISH ===

- [ ] [P1] VIS001 - Add virtual scrolling for large lists. Current List

  viewport). Calculate item positions from fixed height ($list-item-height).

- [ ] [P2] VIS002 - Add custom window title bar. Since Tauri uses frameless

- [ ] [P2] VIS003 - Add smooth scroll momentum and snap-to-item. Lists

- [ ] [P2] VIS004 - Add focus ring animation matching Raycast. When
  navigating list items with keyboard, show a subtle animated focus ring

  Use CSS transition on a pseudo-element that moves between item positions.

- [ ] [P3] VIS005 - Add window open/close animations. When CommandPalette

- [ ] [P3] VIS006 - Add Grid view hover preview tooltip. When hovering a

- [ ] [P3] VIS007 - Add subtle parallax effect to command palette background.

  movement). Creates depth illusion matching Raycast's glass effect. CSS

=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===

- [ ] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  root search and all view search bars.

- [ ] [P1] SRCH002 - Add recent commands tracking. Track last 10 opened
  commands with timestamps. Show "Recent Commands" section in root search
  when query is empty (above other sections). Persist via localStorage/

- [ ] [P2] SRCH003 - Add search bar placeholder cycling. When search bar

  "Type a command...", "Search applications...", "Calculate something...",

- [ ] [P2] SRCH004 - Add ⌘L to copy current item URL/value. Universal

- [ ] [P2] SRCH005 - Add search debouncing and loading state. Debounce

  Cancel pending debounce on unmount.

- [ ] [P3] SRCH006 - Add Tab completion in search. When Tab is pressed in
  search and there's a single matching result, auto-complete the search
  query to match that result's title. If multiple matches share a common
  prefix, complete to that prefix. Visual: briefly highlight the completed
  portion. Works in root search only (not within views). Prevent Tab from
  focusing other elements during search.

  useNavigationStore (stack, push, pop, breadcrumbs — replaces hook),

- [ ] [P2] INFRA008 - Add proper build configuration for production. Vite
  production build: minification (terser), tree-shaking, chunk splitting
  (vendor chunk for React, component chunks per route/view). Configure
  asset hashing for cache busting. Add bundle size analysis (rollup-plugin-
  visualizer). Set up path aliases (@/ for src/). Add environment variable

- [ ] [P2] INFRA009 - Add CI/CD pipeline. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  lint (bun run check), test (bun run test), build (bun run build), optional

- [ ] [P3] INFRA010 - Add telemetry/analytics abstraction. Create

- [ ] [P3] INFRA011 - Add i18n internationalization support. Create

- [ ] [P3] INFRA012 - Add performance monitoring utilities. Create

  re-renders, throttle() and debounce() utilities. Add React.StrictMode
  in dev. Profile List rendering with 500+ items. Identify and fix
  unnecessary re-renders with React.memo where needed. Target: 60fps
  scroll performance.

=== TESTING (NEW) ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST007 - Add unit tests for calculator.ts. Test all math
  operations: basic arithmetic, operator precedence, parentheses, functions
  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,
  edge cases (division by zero, NaN, Infinity), invalid expressions, very

- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.

  empty candidates, special characters, unicode. Minimum 20 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove
  with various types (string, number, boolean, object, array). Test
  validators (isStringArray, isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Test error
  handling (invalid JSON, quota exceeded mock). Test migration paths.
  Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all
  relative date ranges: just now, minutes ago, hours ago, yesterday, N days
  ago, absolute date, different year. Test edge cases: midnight boundaries,
  timezone handling, future dates, invalid dates. Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test
  register/get/search/getSections. Test search matches title, subtitle,
  keywords, aliases. Test section ordering. Test duplicate registration
  warning. Minimum 10 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.
  Flow 1: Open → type query → select result → drill into view → Escape back.
  Flow 2: Open → ⌘K → navigate actions → execute action → verify HUD.
  Flow 3: Settings → change theme → verify visual change.
  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.
  Each flow as separate test. Screenshot on failure.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with
  Playwright to audit each view for WCAG 2.1 AA violations. Test: color
  contrast ratios, ARIA roles/labels, keyboard navigation order, focus
  management, screen reader announcements. Run on: root search, clipboard
  history, snippet manager, emoji picker, settings, forms. Zero violations
  target.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:
  initial render time < 100ms, search filter time < 50ms for 1000 items,
  navigation push/pop < 100ms, scroll FPS > 55fps with 500 items. Use
  vitest + performance.now() for unit benchmarks. Use Playwright for
  runtime performance metrics. Create performance regression baseline.

- [x] [P0] COMP001 - Build Alert/Confirm dialog component. Blocking modal overlay

  alert.tsx and alert.scss. Create useAlert hook in src/hooks/use_alert.ts that

  animations, focus trap (Tab cycling), Escape to dismiss, backdrop click to
  dismiss. useAlert hook with Promise-based confirmAlert() using ref-based
  resolve (no double-resolve). All SCSS values tokenized ($alert-dialog-width,
  $backdrop-blur, $backdrop-overlay added to _tokens.scss). Unique IDs via
  useId(). Stable callback refs to avoid stale closures. Delete action in
  dropdown triggers destructive confirm dialog before showing toast.

  pushed view is a full-screen replacement inside CommandPalette. Escape pops
  the stack. Breadcrumb updates automatically from stack. Animate transitions

  stack instead of viewMode state. Action.Push should be declarative.
  Done: useNavigationStack hook with push/pop/popToRoot, monotonic counter for

  shared BreadcrumbItem type between hook and SearchBar, extracted ColorPickerView
  and DetailView as standalone components, CSS slide transitions in
  command_palette.scss, SearchBar Escape-to-pop with query-clear-first behavior.

+  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,
+  edge cases (division by zero, NaN, Infinity), invalid expressions, very
+  large/small numbers. Minimum 30 test cases. Use vitest.
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
+  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.
+  Test highlighting of matched characters. Test edge cases: empty query,
+  empty candidates, special characters, unicode. Minimum 20 test cases.
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove
+  with various types (string, number, boolean, object, array). Test
+  validators (isStringArray, isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Test error
+  handling (invalid JSON, quota exceeded mock). Test migration paths.
+  Minimum 15 test cases.
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all
+  relative date ranges: just now, minutes ago, hours ago, yesterday, N days
+  ago, absolute date, different year. Test edge cases: midnight boundaries,
+  timezone handling, future dates, invalid dates. Minimum 15 test cases.
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test
+  register/get/search/getSections. Test search matches title, subtitle,
+  keywords, aliases. Test section ordering. Test duplicate registration
+  warning. Minimum 10 test cases.
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.
+  Flow 1: Open → type query → select result → drill into view → Escape back.
+  Flow 2: Open → ⌘K → navigate actions → execute action → verify HUD.
+  Flow 3: Settings → change theme → verify visual change.
+  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.
+  Each flow as separate test. Screenshot on failure.
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with
+  Playwright to audit each view for WCAG 2.1 AA violations. Test: color
+  contrast ratios, ARIA roles/labels, keyboard navigation order, focus
+  management, screen reader announcements. Run on: root search, clipboard
+  history, snippet manager, emoji picker, settings, forms. Zero violations
+  target.
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:
+  initial render time < 100ms, search filter time < 50ms for 1000 items,
+  navigation push/pop < 100ms, scroll FPS > 55fps with 500 items. Use
+  vitest + performance.now() for unit benchmarks. Use Playwright for
+  runtime performance metrics. Create performance regression baseline.

input.prompt:
You are a senior code reviewer. Review the changes made to .claude/progress.txt for task T0001. The task was to "Plan and add minimum 50 tasks to implement real Tauri Raycast clone app."

Read the file /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/.claude/progress.txt (focus on the section after "TAURI DESKTOP APP — NEW TASKS (from T0001 planning)").

Evaluate:
1. Were 50+ new tasks actually added? Count them.
2. Are tasks well-defined with clear acceptance criteria?
3. Are priorities reasonable (P0 for critical Tauri setup, P1 for core features, etc.)?
4. Are there any missing categories or critical Raycast features not covered?
5. Are task IDs unique and non-conflicting with existing ones?
6. Do tasks follow the project conventions (file naming, technology choices)?
7. Any tasks that are too large and should be broken into sub-tasks?

Be thorough. Flag any issues. This is a PR review simulation.

content.text:
Here is my thorough review of the T0001 task output.

---

## PR Review: T0001 -- Plan and add minimum 50 tasks to implement real Tauri Raycast clone app

### 1. Task Count

Counting all new tasks in the "TAURI DESKTOP APP -- NEW TASKS (from T0001 planning)" section (line 960 onward):

| Category | Tasks | IDs |
|---|---|---|
| Tauri Desktop Integration | 10 | TAURI001-TAURI010 |
| Missing Raycast Screens | 6 | SCRN010-SCRN015 |
| Missing UI Components | 9 | COMP016-COMP024 |
| Visual Polish | 7 | VIS001-VIS007 |
| Search & Interaction | 6 | SRCH001-SRCH006 |
| Infrastructure | 9 | INFRA004-INFRA012 |
| Testing (New) | 8 | TEST007-TEST014 |
| **Total** | **55** | |

**PASS**: 55 tasks exceeds the 50-task minimum requirement.

---

### 2. Task Definition Quality and Acceptance Criteria

**Strengths:**
- Every task has a specific description with implementation details (file paths, component names, props, dimensions, keyboard shortcuts).
- Technology choices are explicit (which Tauri plugin, which hook pattern, which directory).
- File naming conventions are specified per task (e.g., `quicklinks_view.tsx`, `fuzzy_search.ts`).
- Most tasks include a "wire demo" or "demo with X" clause, which serves as implicit acceptance criteria.

**Issues:**

- **No formal acceptance criteria format.** Existing completed tasks above use a "Done:" block describing what was accomplished. The new pending tasks lack explicit "Verify:" or "Acceptance:" lines. For example, TAURI002 says "Toggle window show/hide on keypress" but does not state how to verify the edge case ("if window is visible but unfocused, bring to front instead of hiding"). This is an implicit pattern the codebase already follows (completed tasks have detailed "Done:" blocks), but adding explicit verifiable criteria on pending tasks would improve clarity.

- **TAURI005 references TAURI008** ("Store clipboard history in SQLite (TAURI008)") but TAURI005 is P1 and TAURI008 is P2. This dependency is noted but could cause confusion -- the task should clarify the fallback if TAURI008 is not yet complete (e.g., "persist in-memory with localStorage fallback until TAURI008 is done").

- **COMP021 says "No external markdown library -- build minimal parser or use a small one (micromark ~4KB)"** but the project already has `marked` (v17.0.3) and `dompurify` (v3.3.1) installed as dependencies in `package.json`. This task description contradicts the current codebase state. Either the task should say "use existing marked+dompurify" or explicitly state it replaces them.

---

### 3. Priority Assessment

**P0 (Critical path):** TAURI001-TAURI004 -- correctly identified as critical. You cannot have a desktop app without Tauri init, global hotkey, system tray, and window behavior. This is sound.

**P1 (Core features):** TAURI005-TAURI007 (native clipboard, filesystem, app launching), SCRN010-SCRN011 (Quicklinks, Window Management), COMP016-COMP018, SRCH001-SRCH002, INFRA004-INFRA005, VIS001. Reasonable.

**P2 (Important but not blocking):** TAURI008-TAURI010, SCRN012-SCRN014, COMP019-COMP022, VIS002-VIS004, SRCH003-SRCH005, INFRA006-INFRA009. Reasonable.

**P3 (Nice-to-have):** SCRN015, COMP023-COMP024, VIS005-VIS007, SRCH006, INFRA010-INFRA012, THEME003. Appropriate.

**P1000 (Tests):** TEST007-TEST014. All tests are P1000, which is unusual.

**Issues:**

- **VIS001 (virtual scrolling) is P1, which is too high.** Virtual scrolling is a performance optimization. The app works fine without it until there are real datasets with 1000+ items. Given that the app currently uses mock data, this should be P2 at most.

- **INFRA007 (Zustand state management) is P2 but arguably should be P1 or even P0.** The task description itself says App.tsx is 660+ lines and needs decomposition. If INFRA003 (refactor App.tsx, currently P3) and INFRA007 are both delayed, the codebase will accumulate significant technical debt as all these new screens and features get added. These two tasks should be elevated.

- **All test tasks are P1000**, which effectively means "never." At minimum, TEST007 (calculator unit tests) and TEST012 (E2E core flows) should be P1 because they test already-shipped features that could regress as new work lands.

- **INFRA009 (CI/CD) is P2** but should be P1. Without CI, there is no automated quality gate. Given the project has 93 existing tests and strict linting, CI is critical before adding 55 more tasks.

---

### 4. Missing Categories / Critical Raycast Features Not Covered

**Missing entirely:**

- **Confetti / celebration animations.** Raycast has celebratory confetti effects (e.g., completing a daily habit). Minor, but part of Raycast's charm.

- **Menu Bar Commands.** Raycast supports menu bar extras (persistent items in the macOS menu bar showing data like weather, system stats). No tasks cover this.

- **Typeahead / autocomplete in search bar.** Raycast shows inline ghost text autocomplete. SRCH006 covers Tab completion but not the visual ghost text.

- **Raycast AI integration patterns.** SCRN008 covers AI Chat but does not cover AI actions inline in other commands (e.g., "Fix Grammar" action on clipboard text, "Summarize" on selected text). These are core Raycast Pro features.

- **Quicklink dynamic arguments.** SCRN010 mentions `{query}` placeholders but does not cover multiple named arguments or argument validation.

- **Background refresh / extension lifecycle.** There is no task for command lifecycle hooks (onOpen, onClose, willUnmount, background refresh interval). Real Raycast extensions have these.

- **Drag and drop.** Raycast supports dragging items from the list to other apps (e.g., drag a file from File Search to Finder). No task covers this.

- **Multi-select in lists.** Raycast supports selecting multiple items with Shift+click or Ctrl+click for batch actions. Not covered.

---

### 5. Task ID Uniqueness

Existing IDs in use (pre-T0001): L03239-L03670, UXDS001-UXDS003, COMP001-COMP015, SCRN001-SCRN009, THEME001-THEME002, KBD001-KBD003, INFRA001-INFRA003, TEST001-TEST006, T0001, L000X1.

New IDs: TAURI001-TAURI010, SCRN010-SCRN015, COMP016-COMP024, VIS001-VIS007, SRCH001-SRCH006, INFRA004-INFRA012, TEST007-TEST014, THEME003.

**PASS**: All new IDs are unique and non-conflicting. Numbering continues correctly from existing sequences (COMP015 -> COMP016, SCRN009 -> SCRN010, etc.).

---

### 6. Project Convention Compliance

**File naming:** All tasks use snake_case (`quicklinks_view.tsx`, `fuzzy_search.ts`, `use_virtual_scroll.ts`). **PASS**.

**Technology choices:**
- React 19, TypeScript, Sass, Vite -- all tasks comply.
- No mention of CSS modules, styled-components, or Tailwind. **PASS**.
- Tauri v2 correctly specified (`@tauri-apps/cli@next`, `@tauri-apps/plugin-*`). **PASS**.

**Issues:**

- **INFRA007 introduces Zustand.** The project CLAUDE.md says "No over-engineering, no abstractions for single-use cases." Adding Zustand is a new dependency that arguably violates this principle given the project uses React hooks + context. However, it also says App.tsx is 660+ lines, so the refactor is justified. This is a judgment call but should be flagged for discussion.

- **INFRA010 (telemetry/analytics)** adds tracking to a Raycast clone. This is unexpected for a clone project. If this is for internal use, it may be fine, but it should be clarified.

- **INFRA011 (i18n) at P3** is reasonable, but adding 4 translation files when the project is not yet feature-complete adds maintenance burden. Consider deferring to after v1.0.

---

### 7. Tasks That Are Too Large / Should Be Broken Down

Several tasks are oversized based on the pattern established by completed tasks (which were routinely broken into sub-tasks with individual IDs):

1. **TAURI001 (Initialize Tauri v2 project structure)** -- This involves: installing Tauri CLI, running `tauri init`, configuring Cargo.toml, configuring tauri.conf.json (window settings, bundle, allowlist), setting up frameless + transparent window, updating package.json scripts, and verifying dev mode works. That is at least 3-4 sub-tasks. Cargo.toml configuration alone requires understanding Rust toolchain setup.

2. **TAURI005 (Native clipboard access)** -- This task combines: installing Tauri clipboard plugin, writing Rust polling command, emitting events to frontend, implementing content type detection, SQLite integration (cross-task dependency), wiring into ClipboardHistoryView, and managing a 1000-entry cap. Should be at least 4 sub-tasks (plugin setup, Rust polling, frontend integration, storage).

3. **TAURI006 (Native file system access)** -- Combines: plugin setup, Rust recursive search command, directory indexing, file metadata extraction, wiring into FileSearchView, and shell.open integration. At least 3 sub-tasks.

4. **TAURI007 (Native app launching)** -- Combines: macOS app scanning, Windows scanning, icon extraction (base64 PNG from .app bundles is non-trivial), caching, and root search integration. At least 3 sub-tasks.

5. **INFRA004 (Extension architecture)** -- This is a full architectural refactoring: new manifest type, new directory structure, refactoring existing registry, migrating all views to extension folders. Should be at least 3 sub-tasks.

6. **SRCH001 (Fuzzy search)** -- Combines: implementing a scoring algorithm, character highlighting, and applying to all search bars across all views. The algorithm alone is 1 task, the integration is another.

7. **TEST012 (E2E tests with Playwright)** -- Contains 4 independent test flows. Each should be its own sub-task since Playwright setup, configuration, and each flow are distinct units of work.

---

### Summary of Findings

| Criterion | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 50+ tasks added | PASS | 55 tasks |
| Well-defined tasks | PARTIAL | Good detail, but missing explicit acceptance criteria format |
| Priority reasonableness | PARTIAL | VIS001 too high, INFRA003/007/009 too low, TEST P1000 too low for critical tests |
| Missing features covered | PARTIAL | Menu bar commands, drag-and-drop, multi-select, background refresh lifecycle missing |
| Task IDs unique | PASS | All unique and sequential |
| Convention compliance | PASS | File naming, tech stack, Sass all correct |
| Task granularity | PARTIAL | 7 tasks identified as too large, need sub-task breakdown |

**Additional flags:**
- COMP021 contradicts existing `marked` + `dompurify` dependencies.
- TAURI005 has unresolved dependency on TAURI008 (different priority levels).
- INFRA010 (telemetry) is unusual for a clone project and should be justified.

**Recommendation:** Address the priority adjustments, break down the 7 oversized tasks into sub-tasks, add explicit acceptance criteria, and consider adding tasks for the missing Raycast features (menu bar commands, drag-and-drop, multi-select at minimum). The overall planning is solid -- the categories are logical, the technology choices are correct, and the progression from P0 Tauri setup through P3 polish is reasonable.

content.text:
Here is my thorough review of the T0001 task output.

---

## PR Review: T0001 -- Plan and add minimum 50 tasks to implement real Tauri Raycast clone app

### 1. Task Count

Counting all new tasks in the "TAURI DESKTOP APP -- NEW TASKS (from T0001 planning)" section (line 960 onward):

| Category | Tasks | IDs |
|---|---|---|
| Tauri Desktop Integration | 10 | TAURI001-TAURI010 |
| Missing Raycast Screens | 6 | SCRN010-SCRN015 |
| Missing UI Components | 9 | COMP016-COMP024 |
| Visual Polish | 7 | VIS001-VIS007 |
| Search & Interaction | 6 | SRCH001-SRCH006 |
| Infrastructure | 9 | INFRA004-INFRA012 |
| Testing (New) | 8 | TEST007-TEST014 |
| **Total** | **55** | |

**PASS**: 55 tasks exceeds the 50-task minimum requirement.

---

### 2. Task Definition Quality and Acceptance Criteria

**Strengths:**
- Every task has a specific description with implementation details (file paths, component names, props, dimensions, keyboard shortcuts).
- Technology choices are explicit (which Tauri plugin, which hook pattern, which directory).
- File naming conventions are specified per task (e.g., `quicklinks_view.tsx`, `fuzzy_search.ts`).
- Most tasks include a "wire demo" or "demo with X" clause, which serves as implicit acceptance criteria.

**Issues:**

- **No formal acceptance criteria format.** Existing completed tasks above use a "Done:" block describing what was accomplished. The new pending tasks lack explicit "Verify:" or "Acceptance:" lines. For example, TAURI002 says "Toggle window show/hide on keypress" but does not state how to verify the edge case ("if window is visible but unfocused, bring to front instead of hiding"). This is an implicit pattern the codebase already follows (completed tasks have detailed "Done:" blocks), but adding explicit verifiable criteria on pending tasks would improve clarity.

- **TAURI005 references TAURI008** ("Store clipboard history in SQLite (TAURI008)") but TAURI005 is P1 and TAURI008 is P2. This dependency is noted but could cause confusion -- the task should clarify the fallback if TAURI008 is not yet complete (e.g., "persist in-memory with localStorage fallback until TAURI008 is done").

- **COMP021 says "No external markdown library -- build minimal parser or use a small one (micromark ~4KB)"** but the project already has `marked` (v17.0.3) and `dompurify` (v3.3.1) installed as dependencies in `package.json`. This task description contradicts the current codebase state. Either the task should say "use existing marked+dompurify" or explicitly state it replaces them.

---

### 3. Priority Assessment

**P0 (Critical path):** TAURI001-TAURI004 -- correctly identified as critical. You cannot have a desktop app without Tauri init, global hotkey, system tray, and window behavior. This is sound.

**P1 (Core features):** TAURI005-TAURI007 (native clipboard, filesystem, app launching), SCRN010-SCRN011 (Quicklinks, Window Management), COMP016-COMP018, SRCH001-SRCH002, INFRA004-INFRA005, VIS001. Reasonable.

**P2 (Important but not blocking):** TAURI008-TAURI010, SCRN012-SCRN014, COMP019-COMP022, VIS002-VIS004, SRCH003-SRCH005, INFRA006-INFRA009. Reasonable.

**P3 (Nice-to-have):** SCRN015, COMP023-COMP024, VIS005-VIS007, SRCH006, INFRA010-INFRA012, THEME003. Appropriate.

**P1000 (Tests):** TEST007-TEST014. All tests are P1000, which is unusual.

**Issues:**

- **VIS001 (virtual scrolling) is P1, which is too high.** Virtual scrolling is a performance optimization. The app works fine without it until there are real datasets with 1000+ items. Given that the app currently uses mock data, this should be P2 at most.

- **INFRA007 (Zustand state management) is P2 but arguably should be P1 or even P0.** The task description itself says App.tsx is 660+ lines and needs decomposition. If INFRA003 (refactor App.tsx, currently P3) and INFRA007 are both delayed, the codebase will accumulate significant technical debt as all these new screens and features get added. These two tasks should be elevated.

- **All test tasks are P1000**, which effectively means "never." At minimum, TEST007 (calculator unit tests) and TEST012 (E2E core flows) should be P1 because they test already-shipped features that could regress as new work lands.

- **INFRA009 (CI/CD) is P2** but should be P1. Without CI, there is no automated quality gate. Given the project has 93 existing tests and strict linting, CI is critical before adding 55 more tasks.

---

### 4. Missing Categories / Critical Raycast Features Not Covered

**Missing entirely:**

- **Confetti / celebration animations.** Raycast has celebratory confetti effects (e.g., completing a daily habit). Minor, but part of Raycast's charm.

- **Menu Bar Commands.** Raycast supports menu bar extras (persistent items in the macOS menu bar showing data like weather, system stats). No tasks cover this.

- **Typeahead / autocomplete in search bar.** Raycast shows inline ghost text autocomplete. SRCH006 covers Tab completion but not the visual ghost text.

- **Raycast AI integration patterns.** SCRN008 covers AI Chat but does not cover AI actions inline in other commands (e.g., "Fix Grammar" action on clipboard text, "Summarize" on selected text). These are core Raycast Pro features.

- **Quicklink dynamic arguments.** SCRN010 mentions `{query}` placeholders but does not cover multiple named arguments or argument validation.

- **Background refresh / extension lifecycle.** There is no task for command lifecycle hooks (onOpen, onClose, willUnmount, background refresh interval). Real Raycast extensions have these.

- **Drag and drop.** Raycast supports dragging items from the list to other apps (e.g., drag a file from File Search to Finder). No task covers this.

- **Multi-select in lists.** Raycast supports selecting multiple items with Shift+click or Ctrl+click for batch actions. Not covered.

---

### 5. Task ID Uniqueness

Existing IDs in use (pre-T0001): L03239-L03670, UXDS001-UXDS003, COMP001-COMP015, SCRN001-SCRN009, THEME001-THEME002, KBD001-KBD003, INFRA001-INFRA003, TEST001-TEST006, T0001, L000X1.

New IDs: TAURI001-TAURI010, SCRN010-SCRN015, COMP016-COMP024, VIS001-VIS007, SRCH001-SRCH006, INFRA004-INFRA012, TEST007-TEST014, THEME003.

**PASS**: All new IDs are unique and non-conflicting. Numbering continues correctly from existing sequences (COMP015 -> COMP016, SCRN009 -> SCRN010, etc.).

---

### 6. Project Convention Compliance

**File naming:** All tasks use snake_case (`quicklinks_view.tsx`, `fuzzy_search.ts`, `use_virtual_scroll.ts`). **PASS**.

**Technology choices:**
- React 19, TypeScript, Sass, Vite -- all tasks comply.
- No mention of CSS modules, styled-components, or Tailwind. **PASS**.
- Tauri v2 correctly specified (`@tauri-apps/cli@next`, `@tauri-apps/plugin-*`). **PASS**.

**Issues:**

- **INFRA007 introduces Zustand.** The project CLAUDE.md says "No over-engineering, no abstractions for single-use cases." Adding Zustand is a new dependency that arguably violates this principle given the project uses React hooks + context. However, it also says App.tsx is 660+ lines, so the refactor is justified. This is a judgment call but should be flagged for discussion.

- **INFRA010 (telemetry/analytics)** adds tracking to a Raycast clone. This is unexpected for a clone project. If this is for internal use, it may be fine, but it should be clarified.

- **INFRA011 (i18n) at P3** is reasonable, but adding 4 translation files when the project is not yet feature-complete adds maintenance burden. Consider deferring to after v1.0.

---

### 7. Tasks That Are Too Large / Should Be Broken Down

Several tasks are oversized based on the pattern established by completed tasks (which were routinely broken into sub-tasks with individual IDs):

1. **TAURI001 (Initialize Tauri v2 project structure)** -- This involves: installing Tauri CLI, running `tauri init`, configuring Cargo.toml, configuring tauri.conf.json (window settings, bundle, allowlist), setting up frameless + transparent window, updating package.json scripts, and verifying dev mode works. That is at least 3-4 sub-tasks. Cargo.toml configuration alone requires understanding Rust toolchain setup.

2. **TAURI005 (Native clipboard access)** -- This task combines: installing Tauri clipboard plugin, writing Rust polling command, emitting events to frontend, implementing content type detection, SQLite integration (cross-task dependency), wiring into ClipboardHistoryView, and managing a 1000-entry cap. Should be at least 4 sub-tasks (plugin setup, Rust polling, frontend integration, storage).

3. **TAURI006 (Native file system access)** -- Combines: plugin setup, Rust recursive search command, directory indexing, file metadata extraction, wiring into FileSearchView, and shell.open integration. At least 3 sub-tasks.

4. **TAURI007 (Native app launching)** -- Combines: macOS app scanning, Windows scanning, icon extraction (base64 PNG from .app bundles is non-trivial), caching, and root search integration. At least 3 sub-tasks.

5. **INFRA004 (Extension architecture)** -- This is a full architectural refactoring: new manifest type, new directory structure, refactoring existing registry, migrating all views to extension folders. Should be at least 3 sub-tasks.

6. **SRCH001 (Fuzzy search)** -- Combines: implementing a scoring algorithm, character highlighting, and applying to all search bars across all views. The algorithm alone is 1 task, the integration is another.

7. **TEST012 (E2E tests with Playwright)** -- Contains 4 independent test flows. Each should be its own sub-task since Playwright setup, configuration, and each flow are distinct units of work.

---

### Summary of Findings

| Criterion | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 50+ tasks added | PASS | 55 tasks |
| Well-defined tasks | PARTIAL | Good detail, but missing explicit acceptance criteria format |
| Priority reasonableness | PARTIAL | VIS001 too high, INFRA003/007/009 too low, TEST P1000 too low for critical tests |
| Missing features covered | PARTIAL | Menu bar commands, drag-and-drop, multi-select, background refresh lifecycle missing |
| Task IDs unique | PASS | All unique and sequential |
| Convention compliance | PASS | File naming, tech stack, Sass all correct |
| Task granularity | PARTIAL | 7 tasks identified as too large, need sub-task breakdown |

**Additional flags:**
- COMP021 contradicts existing `marked` + `dompurify` dependencies.
- TAURI005 has unresolved dependency on TAURI008 (different priority levels).
- INFRA010 (telemetry) is unusual for a clone project and should be justified.

**Recommendation:** Address the priority adjustments, break down the 7 oversized tasks into sub-tasks, add explicit acceptance criteria, and consider adding tasks for the missing Raycast features (menu bar commands, drag-and-drop, multi-select at minimum). The overall planning is solid -- the categories are logical, the technology choices are correct, and the progression from P0 Tauri setup through P3 polish is reasonable.

Excellent review. Let me address the key findings:

1. **COMP021 contradicts existing `marked` + `dompurify` deps** — need to fix
2. **Priority adjustments** — VIS001 P1→P2, INFRA009 P2→P1
3. **TAURI005/TAURI008 dependency** — add fallback note
4. **Large tasks need sub-task breakdown** — these will be broken down when they start (per WORKFLOW step 3: "Evaluate scope: if task involves more than ~3 files, break it into smaller sub-tasks BEFORE starting")
